Then Complementary of 41o = 90o - 41o = 49o

questions.question: What is the measure of the supplement of 54o?
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are Supplementary Angles, if they add up to 180°
Here first angle = 54o 
Then Supplementary of 54o = 180o - 54o = 126o

questions.question: Identify which of the following pairs of angles are complementary.
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are Complementary Angles, if they add up to 90°
Here, First angle = 63o 
Second angle = 27o 
Then, 63o + 27o = 90o 
Thus, these angles are Complementary Angles.

questions.question: Identify which of the following pairs of angles are supplementary.
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are Supplementary Angles, if they add up to 180°
Here, First angle = 112o 
Second angle = 68o 
Then, 112o + 68o = 180o 
Thus, These angles are Supplementary Angles.

questions.question: Find the angle, which is equal to its complement.
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
But the angle is equal to its complement so, the complement of  x = x 
x + x = 90o 
2x ={tex}\frac{90^o}{2}{/tex}
Then, 45o is equal to its complement.

questions.question: Angles which are both supplementary and vertically opposite are
questions.explanation: When the sum of the measures of two angles is 180o, the angles are called supplementary angles.

questions.question: Find the angle, which is equal to its supplement.
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are Supplementary Angles, if they add up to 180°
Let the angle be x.
Then the supplement of the angle is also x.
x + x = 180o 
2x = 180o 
x = 90o 
Thus, 90{tex}^\circ {/tex} is equal to its supplement.

questions.question: Lines l{tex}\parallel {/tex}m, t is a transversal then {tex}\angle {/tex}x = ?
questions.explanation: as l{tex}\parallel {/tex}m, 120{tex}^\circ {/tex} and x are corresponding angles. Because of corresponding angles property, x= 120{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.explanation:
Here, 60{tex}^\circ {/tex} + z = 180{tex}^\circ {/tex} ￼￼(sum of Co interior angles) 
z = 180{tex}^\circ {/tex} - 60{tex}^\circ {/tex} = 120{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.question: Lines l {tex}\parallel {/tex} m; t is a transversal {tex}\angle {/tex} x = ?
questions.explanation: Because of alternate interier angles  {tex}\angle {/tex}x = 60{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.question: The angle which is four times its complement is
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
Second angle = 4x
Complementary angles = x + 4x = 90o 
5x = 90o 
x = 18o 
4x = 4(18o) = 72o

questions.question: If arms of two angles are parallel, if {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = 70°, then find the {tex}\angle {/tex}DEF.
questions.explanation:
AB ∥  DE and BC is transversal line and {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = 70°
{tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle {/tex} DGC (corresponding angles)
So, {tex}\angle {/tex}DGC = 70°
Now BC ∥ EF and DE is transversal line and {tex}\angle {/tex}DGC = 70°
{tex}\angle {/tex}DGC = {tex}\angle {/tex}DEF (corresponding angle)
So, {tex}\angle {/tex}DEF = 70°

questions.question: The angle which is five times its supplement is:
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
Second angle = 5x
Then supplementary angles = x + 5x = 180o
6x = 180o 
x = 30o 
Then, 5x = 5(30o) = 150o

questions.question: Find x if l{tex}\parallel {/tex}m.
questions.explanation:
Here, x & 2x are co- interior angles and their sum is 180o
Then x + 2x = 180{tex}^\circ {/tex} 
3x = 180{tex}^\circ {/tex} 
x = 60{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.question: Find the value of x if l{tex}\parallel {/tex}m.
questions.explanation:
Let the angle adjacent to 110 degree angle be y. Now, y makes a linear pair with 110 degree. So, 110o + y = 180o​

Therefore y = 70{tex}^\circ {/tex} 
Now, x & y are alternate interior angles hence x+y=180{tex}^\circ {/tex} 
x = 180{tex}^\circ {/tex} - 110{tex}^\circ {/tex}=70{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.question: The angle which makes a linear pair with an angle of 61o is of:
questions.explanation:
A linear pair is a pair of adjacent angles whose non-common sides are opposite rays. We know that a measure of sum of adjacent angles is equal to 180o. Let the measure of other angles be xo.
Then,
x + 61o = 180o
x = 180o - 61o
x = 119o

90o = 90o
LHS = RHS

questions.question: Find the angle which is equal to its complement.
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are complementary if their sum is 90o 
If one angle is x then the other can be 90-x. When we take x = 45 degree than 90 - 45 will also be equal to the first angle. In this way, 45 degree angle is the complementary angle for itself.

questions.question: Find the angle which is equal to its supplement.
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are supplementary if their sum is 180o 
Then, let the first angle be x. then its Supplement will be 180 - x. This will be only possible when x = 90 degrees. Because 90 + 90 = 180 degrees.

questions.question: Find the measure of an angle which is 24o more than its complement.
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
Second angle = x + 24o
Complementary angle= x + x + 24o = 90o
2x + 24o = 90o
2x = 90o - 24o
2x = 66o
x = 33o

questions.question: Find the measure of an angle which is 32o less than its complement.
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
Second angle = 32o less than = x - 32o 
Complementary angle = x + x - 32o = 90o 
2x - 32o = 90o 
2x = 90o + 32o 
2x = 122o 
x = 61o 
x - 32o = 61o - 32o = 29o

questions.question: How many end points does a line segment have?
questions.explanation: A line segment is just part of a line. It has two endpoints, one from where it starts and one where it ends.

questions.question: In the following figure: Which angle is adjacent to {tex}\angle {/tex}1?

So {tex}\angle {/tex}2 and {tex}\angle {/tex}5 are adjacent angles of {tex}\angle {/tex}1 because of common vertex and common arm.

questions.question: If a line is a transversal to three lines, how many points of intersections are there?
questions.explanation: If a line is a transversal to three lines, 3 points of intersections are there because the given line will cut other three lines at 3 points.

questions.question: When the sum of the measures of two angles is 90{tex}^\circ {/tex}, the angles are called ________.
questions.explanation:
Two angles are called complementary angles, if their sum is one right angle i.e. 90°.
Each angle is called the complement of the other.

questions.question: ________ angles have a common vertex and a common arm but no common interior points.
questions.explanation:
Two angles are said to be adjacent angles, if;

  1. they have a common vertex,
  2. they have a common arm
  3. the other arms of the two angles lie on opposite sides of the common arm and
  4. no common interior points

questions.question: When two lines intersect, the vertically opposite angles so formed are _______.
questions.explanation: When two lines intersect each other, the vertically opposite angles so formed are Equal.

questions.question: Two lines l and m intersect if they have a point in _____.
questions.explanation: Two lines l and m intersect if they have a point in common and this point will be the point of intersection.

questions.question: When a transversal cuts two lines, such that pairs of corresponding angles are equal, then the lines have to be _______.
questions.explanation:
According to converse of Corresponding Angle Axiom.
If pair of corresponding angles are equal then the given lines are parallel to each other.

questions.question: How many end points does a line have?
questions.explanation:
In geometry a line:

  • is straight (no curves),
  • has no thickness, and
  • extends in both directions without end (infinitely).

A line has no length and extends infinitely in both directions.

questions.question: In the following figure: Which angle is adjacent to {tex}\angle {/tex}AOC?
questions.explanation:
In the figure:


{tex}\angle {/tex}COE is adjacent to {tex}\angle {/tex}AOC means {tex}\angle {/tex}1 is adjacent to {tex}\angle {/tex}2 because they share a common vertex

questions.question: If a line is a transversal to two lines, how many points of intersections are there?
questions.explanation: If a line is a transversal to two lines, 2 points of intersections are there because the given line will intersect the other two lines at 2 different points.

questions.question: State the property that is used below: If {tex}\angle{/tex}4 = {tex}\angle{/tex}6, then a {tex}\parallel {/tex} b.
questions.explanation:
If {tex}\angle{/tex}4 = {tex}\angle{/tex}6, then a {tex}\parallel {/tex} b then, it uses alternate interior angles property

here, alternate interior angles are : {tex}\angle{/tex}4 = {tex}\angle{/tex}6 ; {tex}\angle{/tex}3 = {tex}\angle{/tex}5

questions.question: Whenever two angles are complementary, each angle is said to be the ________ of the other angle.
questions.explanation: Whenever two angles are complementary there sum is 90 degrees and each angle is said to be the complement of the other angle.

questions.question: A _______ is a pair of adjacent angles whose non-common sides are opposite rays.
questions.explanation: linear pair are supplementary because they always add to 180° and because they are adjacent, the two non-common legs form a straight line segment.

questions.question: A line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points is called a ______.
questions.explanation: A line intersecting two or more given lines in a plane at different points is called a transversal to the given line.

questions.question: When a transversal cuts two lines, such that pairs of _________ are equal, the lines have to be parallel.
questions.explanation:
When the transversal intersects two parallel lines:

  • Pairs of corresponding angles are equal.
  • Pairs of alternate angles are equal
  • Interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary.

questions.question: How many end points a ray have?
questions.explanation: A ray is a part of a line that has one endpoint and goes on infinitely in only one direction. You cannot measure the length of a ray.

questions.question: In the following figure: Which angle is vertically opposite to {tex}\angle{/tex}4?
questions.explanation:
In the figure:

Vertically opposite angles are: {tex}\angle {/tex}1 = {tex}\angle {/tex}4 ; {tex}\angle {/tex}5 = {tex}\angle {/tex}2 +{tex}\angle {/tex}3
Then, vertically opposite to {tex}\angle {/tex}4 = {tex}\angle {/tex}1

questions.question: If a line is a transversal to one line, how many points of intersections are there?
questions.explanation: When a line cuts another line at a point then that point will be the point of intersection. Similarly when a transversal cuts another transversal there will be only one point of contact between them and this will also be the point of intersection

questions.question: State the property that is used below: If {tex}\angle{/tex}4 + {tex}\angle{/tex}5 = 180°, then a ∥ b.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}4 + {tex}\angle{/tex}5 are interior angles
If the transversal cuts across parallel lines then the interior angles are supplementary (add to 180°).
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}4 + {tex}\angle{/tex}5 = 180°
If a transversal intersects two lines so that interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary, then the lines are parallel.
And a ∥ b

questions.question: The complement of angle 30o is ________.
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are complementary angle, if they adds up to 90o 
Then, the complement of angle 30o is = 90o - 30o = 60o

questions.question: The angles in a linear pair are ________.
questions.explanation: The angles in a linear pair are supplementary because their sum is 180 degrees. Angle A and angle B are supplementary angles

questions.question: When a transversal cuts two lines, such that pairs of interior angles on the same side of the transversal are _______, the lines have to be parallel.
questions.explanation:
For line AB and CD with transversal PS
{tex}\angle{/tex}AQP = {tex}\angle{/tex}CRQ (corresponding angles) ...(i)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AQP + {tex}\angle{/tex}AQR = 180°...(ii)
Putting (i) into (ii)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AQP + {tex}\angle{/tex}CRQ = 180°
Similarly, {tex}\angle{/tex}BQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}DRQ = 180°
When the transversal intersects two parallel lines then Interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary.

questions.question: If we extend the two end points in either direction endlessly, we get a _______.
questions.explanation: A line is a collection of points along a straight path that goes on and on in opposite directions. A line has no endpoints. So, length of a line cannot be measured.

questions.question: In the following figure: What is the vertically opposite angle of {tex}\angle{/tex}5?
questions.explanation:
In the figure:

angle {tex}\angle {/tex}AOD is lying opposite to the {tex}\angle {/tex}COB in the vertical direction. Therefore the two angles are vertically opposite to each other {tex}\angle {/tex}COB  = {tex}\angle {/tex}2 + {tex}\angle {/tex}3.

questions.question: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, each pair of _______ are equal.
questions.explanation:
When two lines are crossed by another line (which is called the Transversal), the pairs of angles

  • opposite sides of the transversal
  • but inside the two lines

are called Alternate Interior Angles.
When the two lines are parallel Alternate Interior Angles are equal.

 {tex}\angle{/tex}4 =  {tex}\angle{/tex}6 and  {tex}\angle{/tex}3 =  {tex}\angle{/tex}5

questions.question: When two angles are supplementary, each angle is said to be the ________ of the other.
questions.explanation: When two angles are supplementary, each angle is said to be the supplement of the other. In the given figure, angle a is the supplement of angle b and a + b = 180 degree

questions.question: A pair of supplementary angles form a _______ when placed adjacent to each other.
questions.explanation: A pair of supplementary angles form a Linear Pair when placed adjacent to each other. Here 40-degree angle and 140-degree angle makes a sum of 180 degrees.

questions.question: Alternate interior angles have different vertices are on opposite sides of the _______ and lie ‘between’ the two lines.
questions.explanation:
In this figure, the angles marked in red are alternate interior angles.
When two lines are crossed by another line (which is called the Transversal), the pairs of angles

  • on opposite sides of the transversal
  • but inside the two lines

are called Alternate Interior Angles.
When the two lines are parallel Alternate Interior Angles are equal.

questions.question: An ________ is formed when lines or line segments meet.
questions.explanation: An Angle is formed when lines or line segments meet at a common point called its vertex.

questions.question: In the following figure: Which angle is adjacent to {tex}\angle{/tex}3?
questions.explanation:
Two angles are Adjacent when they have a common side and a common vertex (corner point) and don't overlap.
In this fig. 

{tex}\angle{/tex}4 is adjacent to {tex}\angle{/tex}3

questions.question: If two _________ are cut by a transversal, each pair of corresponding angles are equal in measure.
questions.explanation: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, each pair of corresponding angles are equal in measure because they are intersected by the same transversal line with the same inclination.

questions.question: Two ________ form a linear pair.
questions.explanation:
If two angles form a linear pair, the angles are supplementary, so you have 2 angles whose measures add to 180°, which means they are supplementary. If two angles form a linear pair, the angles are right angles.
90° + 90°= 180°

questions.question: Parallel lines are lines on a plane that ______ anywhere.
questions.explanation: Two lines in the same plane are said to be parallel if they do not meet anywhere, no matter how long they are. The difference between two parallel lines remains constant, i.e. the difference neither increases not decrease. Hence the parallel lines never meet anywhere.

questions.question: Find the angle which is five times its complement.
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
Second angle = 5x
Complementary angles adds up to 90o 
Then, x + 5x = 90o 
6x = 90o 
x = 15o and 5x = 5(15o) = 75o

questions.question: Find the angle which is five times its supplement.
questions.explanation:
Let first angle = x
Second angle = 5x
We know that supplementary angle are 180o 
Then, x + 5x = 180o 
6x = 180o 
x = 30o 
Its supplementary 5x = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 30o = 150o

questions.question: Two supplementary angles are in the ratio 3 : 2. Find the angles.
questions.explanation:
Two supplementary angles are in the ratio 3 : 2.
Let first angle = 3x
Second angle = 2x
Then , 3x + 2x = 180o 
5x = 180o 
x = 36o 
Then angles are = 3x = 3(36o) = 108o and 2x = 2(36o) = 72o

questions.question: Two complementary angles are in the ratio 4 : 5. Find the angles.
questions.explanation:
Two complementary angles are in the ratio 4 : 5.
Let first angle = 4x
Second angle = 5x
Then, 4x + 5x = 90o
9x = 90o
x = 10o
Then angles are = 4x = 4(10o) = 40o and 5x = 5(10o) = 50o

questions.question: Which pair of the following angles are supplementary?
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are supplementary when they add up to 180 degrees.
Here, first angle = 110o
Second angle = 70o
Then, 110o + 70o = 180o
Thus, these Angles are Supplementary.

questions.question: Which pairs of following angles are complementary?
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles are complementary when they add up to 90 degrees.
Here, first angle = 32o
Second angle = 58o
Then, 32o + 58o = 90o
Thus, these angles are complementary.

questions.question: In fig, if PQ {tex}\parallel {/tex} ST, {tex}\angle {/tex}PQR = 110o and {tex}\angle {/tex}RST = 130o, then find the value of {tex}\angle {/tex}QRS.
questions.explanation:
Draw a line parallel to ST through point R.

{tex}\angle {/tex}PQR + {tex}\angle {/tex}QRX = 180o ( co-interior angles on the same side of transversal QR)
110o + {tex}\angle {/tex}QRX = 180o
{tex}\angle {/tex}QRX = 70o
also, 
{tex}\angle {/tex}RST + {tex}\angle {/tex}SRY = 180​​​​​​​o ( co-interior angles on the same side of transversal SR)
130{tex}^\circ {/tex} + {tex}\angle {/tex}SRY = 180​​​​​​​o
{tex}\angle {/tex}SRY = 50o
XY is a straight line. RQ and RS stand on it.​​​​​​​
{tex}\angle {/tex}QRX + {tex}\angle {/tex}QRS + {tex}\angle {/tex}SRY = 180​​​​​​​o
70​​​​​​​o + {tex}\angle {/tex}QRS + 50​​​​​​​o = 180​​​​​​​o
{tex}\angle {/tex}QRS = 180​​​​​​​o - 120​​​​​​​o = 60​​​​​​​o​​​​​​

questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} of right angle = {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 90o = 54o
Then, First angle = 54o
Supplementary of first angle = 180o - 54o = 126o

questions.explanation:

questions.question: Find the complement of {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} of right angle.

Here, {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}of right angle = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 90o = 60o
Then, First angle = 60o
Second angle = 90o - 60o = 30o

questions.question: Find the complement of 58o.
questions.explanation:
We know that Two Angles are Complementary when they add up to 90 degrees.
Then, complement of 58o = 90o - 58o = 32o

questions.question: Find the complement of {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of right angle.
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}of right angle = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 90o = 45o 
Then, First angle = 45o
Second angle = 90o - 45o = 45o

questions.question: Find the supplement of {tex}\frac{1}{{15}}{/tex} of right angle.
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\frac{1}{{15}}{/tex} of right angle = {tex}\frac{1}{{15}}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 90o = 6o 
Then, First angle = 6o 
Second angle = 90o - 6o = 84o

questions.question: How many medians a triangle can have?
questions.explanation:
A median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposing side.
Every triangle has exactly three medians, one from each vertex and they all intersect each other at the triangle's centroid.

questions.question: A _________ connects a vertex of a triangle to the mid-point of the opposite side.
questions.explanation:
A median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposing side.
Every triangle has exactly three medians, one from each vertex and they all intersect each other at the triangle's centroid.

questions.question: How many altitudes can a triangle have ?
questions.explanation:
Every triangle has three bases (any of its sides) and three altitudes(heights).
Every altitude is the perpendicular segment from a vertex to its opposite side (or the extension of the opposite side)

questions.question: Angle opposite to the side LM of {tex}\triangle {/tex}LMN is
questions.explanation: The angle opposite to any side of a triangle is always the angle between the other two sides.

questions.question: The side opposite to the vertex Q of {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR is
questions.explanation: The side opposite to any vertex of a triangle is the side joining two other vertexes.

questions.question: Vertex opposite to the side RT of {tex}\triangle {/tex}RST is
questions.explanation: Vertex opposite to any side of the triangle is just the facing vertex and not the vertex that is lying on the given line.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle {/tex} PQR, PM is
questions.explanation: If the line from the vertex to the opposite side makes 90-degree angle( or perpendicular ) with the side, then it is altitude.

questions.question: A triangle in which two sides are of equal length is called ________ triangle.
questions.explanation:
Triangle having all sides equal is the Equilateral triangle.
Triangle having two sides equal is an isosceles triangle.
Triangle having all sides of different length is a scalene triangle.

questions.question: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side of the triangle.
questions.explanation: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side of the triangle.

questions.question: In the Pythagoras theorem, the triangle must be ________ triangle.
questions.explanation: Pythagoras theorem holds true only for Right-angled triangle, i.e. one angle is 90°

And in an equilateral triangle, each angle is 60 degree.
x + x + x = 180, 3x = 180
thus, x = 180/3 = 60

questions.question: Find the value of x in given figure.
questions.explanation:

Thus, 120°=60°+x

x=60°

questions.question: {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC is right-angled at C. If AC = 5 cm and BC = 12 cm find the length of AB.
questions.explanation:
As per, Pythagoras theorem, AC2 + BC2 = AB2
Thus, 52 + 122 = AB2
This gives, AB2 = 132 that implies, AB = 13cm

questions.question: {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR is a triangle right-angled at P. If PQ = 3 cm and PR = 4 cm, find QR.
questions.explanation:
As per pythagorus theorem, PQ2 + PR2 = QR2
So, 32 + 42 = QR2
QR2 = 25, QR = 5 cm

questions.question: Which is the longest side in the triangle PQR right angled at P
questions.explanation:
In any triangle the angle opposite the greater side is greater.
In the triangle PQR right angled at P, so angle p is larger angle.
So side opposite to angle P is QR is longest side of triangle PQR.

questions.question: Which is the longest side in the triangle ABC right angled at B?
questions.explanation:

In the triangle, ABC right angled at B, so angle B is larger angle.
So side opposite to angle B is AC is longest side of triangle ABC.

questions.question: Which is the longest side of a right triangle?

questions.question: Find angle of x in below figure.
questions.explanation:
It is an isosceles triangle, So angles opposite to equal sides will also be equal.
And, we also know, the sum of adjacent angles is 180°
So, x + 120 = 180°
x = 60°

questions.question: How many altitudes does a triangle have?
questions.explanation:
Every altitude is the perpendicular segment from a vertex to its opposite side.
Thus, a triangle can have 3 altitudes.

questions.question: Write the side which is opposite to the vertex B of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC.
questions.explanation:
Leaving the given vertex i.e. B ,

line segment joining other two vertex is opposite side to the given vertex.

so, AC would be opposite to vertex B

questions.question: A triangle in which two altitudes of the triangle are two of its sides is ________.
questions.explanation:
A triangle in which two altitudes of the triangle are two of its sides is right-angled triangle
It has perpendicular and base as two altitudes.

questions.question: A ________ is a simple closed curve made of three line segments.
questions.explanation: A polygon with three sides is called a triangle. A triangle has three sides and three angles and three vertices.

questions.question: An ________ has one end point at a vertex of the triangle and the other on the line opposite side which meets opposite side perpendicularly.
questions.explanation: The altitude is a line segment that extends from a vertex and that is perpendicular to the side opposite the vertex.

questions.question: The sum of interior opposite angles is ________, when the exterior angle is right angle.
questions.explanation: Sum of two opposite interior angles is equal to exterior angle.

questions.question: The sum of the length of any two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side.
questions.explanation: The Triangle Inequality Theorem states that the sum of any 2 sides of a triangle must be greater than the measure of the third side.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 70o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 25o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
As we know
Property 1: Sum of two opposite interior angles is equal to the exterior angle.
Property 2: Sum of all angles of the triangle is 180°
Using 1, 70 = 25 + x, x = 45°
Now, using 2, 45 + 25 + y = 180°
y = 110°

questions.question: Write the angle opposite to the side LM of {tex}\triangle {/tex}LMN.
questions.explanation: Consider  {tex}\triangle {/tex}LMN. The  {tex}\angle {/tex}N of triangle LMN is the point of intersection of side LN and MN.LM is remaining third side. So {tex}\angle {/tex}N is opposite to side LM.

questions.question: According to Pythagoras property, in a right-angled triangle, the square of the ______ = sum of the squares of the base and perpendicular
questions.explanation:
Pythagoras Theorem states that the square of the hypotenuse in a right angled triangle is equal to the sum of the square of the other two sides.
(Hypotenuse)2 = (base)2 + (perpendicular)2

questions.question: A triangle has _________ vertices, three sides and three angles.
questions.explanation: A polygon with three sides is called a triangle. A triangle has three sides and three angles and three vertices.

questions.question: Through each vertex, an ________ can be drawn.
questions.explanation: An altitude has one end point at a vertex of the triangle and the other on the line containing the opposite side. Through each vertex, an altitude can be drawn.

questions.question: A triangle in which all three sides are of equal length is called an ________ triangle.
questions.explanation: A triangle in which have all its three sides equal in length is called an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle all sides are of ________ length.
questions.explanation: A triangle in which have all its three sides equal in length is called an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 60o and 80o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
The exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of its interior opposite angles.
Exterior angle of a triangle = sum of interior angles.
Exterior angle of triangle = 60 + 80 = 140°

questions.question: The lengths of two sides of a triangle are 6 cm and 8 cm. Between which two numbers can length of the third side fall?

options.html: greater than 2 and less than 14 cm
greater than 5 and less than 14 cm
greater than 5 and less than 10 cm
greater than 2 and less than 12 cm

questions.explanation:
Sum of given two sides = 6 cm + 8 cm = 14 cm
Hence, the third side should always be less than 14 cm.
The difference between given sides = 8 cm – 6 cm = 2 cm
Hence, the third side should be more than 2 cm
So, the range of measurements of third side = 2 cm to 14 cm.

questions.question: Write the vertex opposite to the side RT of {tex}\triangle {/tex}RST.
questions.explanation: Consider  {tex}\triangle {/tex}RST, the Angle S of triangle RST is the point of intersection of side RS and ST. RT is remaining third side. So vertex S and angle S is opposite to side RT.

questions.question: Write the vertex opposite to the side PR of {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR.
questions.explanation: Consider  {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR, the angle Q of triangle PQR is the point of intersection of side PQ and QR. PR is remaining third side. So vertex Q and angle Q is opposite to side PR.

questions.question: An ________ of a triangle is equal to the sum of its interior opposite angles.
questions.explanation:
If a side of a triangle is produced, then the exterior angle so formed is equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles.

In triangle PQR, QR is produced to point S. where angle PQR is exterior angle of triangle PQR.
For line QS
{tex}\angle{/tex}3 + {tex}\angle{/tex}4 = 180 ° (linear pair) ----- (i)
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 = 180 ( Angle sum property of triangle )----(ii)
From (i) and (ii)
{tex}\angle{/tex}3 + {tex}\angle{/tex}4 = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}4 = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2

questions.question: The sum of all three angles of a triangle is ___________.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\triangle{/tex} PQR with angles {tex}\angle{/tex}1, {tex}\angle{/tex}2 and {tex}\angle{/tex}3.
Draw a line XY passing through P parallel to QR
For lines XY and QR with transversal PQ
{tex}\angle{/tex}2 = {tex}\angle{/tex}4 (alternate angles) …….(i)
For lines XY and QR with transversal PR
{tex}\angle{/tex}3 = {tex}\angle{/tex}5 (alternate angles) …….(ii)

Also, for line XY
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}4 + {tex}\angle{/tex}5 = 180° (linear pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 = 180° [from (i) and (ii)]
So, The sum of all three angles of a triangle is 180°.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle each angle has measure ________.
questions.explanation:
An equilateral triangle is a triangle in which all three sides and angle are equal.
The sum of the angles in a triangle is 180°.
If x is measurement of angle, so
x + x + x = 180°
3x = 180°
x = 180° {tex}\div{/tex} 3
x = 60°

questions.question: A triangle in which two sides are of equal length is called an ________ triangle.
questions.explanation: Triangle with two sides of equal length are called isosceles triangle and the two angles opposite to sides of equal length.

questions.question: Two angles of a triangle are 30º and 80º. Find the third angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of an triangle is 180°
So, the third angle would be,
180 - (30 + 80) = 70°

questions.question: How many medians can a triangle have?
questions.explanation:
A median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposing side.
Every triangle has exactly three medians.

questions.question: If the Pythagoras property holds for some triangle, the triangle will be:
questions.explanation: The Pythagorean Theorem applies only to right triangles. So, if the Pythagoras property holds for some triangle, the triangle will be right-angled triangle.

questions.question: A ________ connects a vertex of a triangle to the mid-point of the opposite side.
questions.explanation: A median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.A triangle therefore has three medians.

questions.question: Exterior angles can be formed for a triangle in ________ ways.
questions.explanation: An exterior angle of a triangle is formed when any side is extended outwards.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle ________ sides have same length.
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle is a triangle that has (at least) two equal side lengths and the angles opposite the equal sides are also equal.

questions.question: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the _________.
questions.explanation: The Triangle Inequality Theorem states that the sum of any 2 sides of a triangle must be greater than the measure of the third side.

questions.question: One of the angles of a triangle is 80º and the other two angles are equal. Find the measure of each of the equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of the triangle is 180°
Let each angle be x
So, 80 + 2x = 180°
2x = 100°
x = 50°

questions.question: The lengths of two sides of a triangle are 12 cm and 15 cm. Between what two measures should the length of the third side fall?

options.html: greater than 2 and less than 30 cm
greater than 9 and less than 27 cm
greater than 3 and less than 27 cm

questions.explanation:
Sum of given two sides = 12 cm + 15 cm = 27 cm
Hence, the third side should always be less than 27 cm.
The difference between given sides = 15 cm – 12 cm = 3 cm
Hence, the third side should be more than 3 cm
So, the range of measure of third side = 3 cm to 27 cm.

questions.question: An ________ is formed when lines or line segments meet.
questions.explanation: An angle is defined as a figure that is formed from two lines segments that meet at a common place. The sides of a triangle are examples of lines that form an angle.

questions.question: Determine th type of triangle, given length of sides are 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm ________.
questions.explanation: 32 + 42 = 52 (pythagoras theorem)

questions.question: A median wholly lie in the __________ of the triangle.
questions.explanation: A median of triangle is a line segment that joins a vertex to the mid-point of the side that is opposite to that vertex. In all types of triangles, the median do lie always in the interior of the triangle. Which can show the following figure are

questions.question: The sum of an exterior angle of a triangle and its adjacent interior angle is _________.
questions.explanation: If one side of a triangle is extended beyond the vertex, an exterior angle is formed. This exterior angle is supplementary means 180 degree with its adjacent, linear angle. Since the angle sum in a triangle is also 180 degrees, the exterior angle must have a measure equal to the sum of the remaining angles, called the remote interior angles. So, The sum of an exterior angle of a triangle and its adjacent interior angle is 180 degree.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, angles opposite to equal sides are ________.
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle is a triangle that has (at least) two equal side lengths and the angles opposite the equal sides are also equal.

questions.question: The side opposite to the right angle is called the _________ of the right-angled triangle.
questions.explanation: In a right-angled triangle, the side opposite to the right angle is called the hypotenuse and the other two sides are called its legs or arms.

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 1:2:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of triangle is 180°
Let x be an angle,
So, x + 2x + x = 180°,
x = 45°,
2x = 90°

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 3:2:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Let x be an angle,
3x + 2x + x = 180°(sum of all angles of triangle is 180°)
6x = 180°
x = 30°, 2x = 60°, 3x = 90°

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 4:5:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Let x be an angle
4x + 5x + x = 180°(sum of all angles of triangle is 180°)
10x = 180
x = 18°, 4x = 72°, 5x = 90°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 35o and {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 65o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}C is
questions.explanation:
The sum of all the interior angles of a triangle equals 180°.
{tex}\angle {/tex}A + {tex}\angle {/tex}B +{tex}\angle {/tex} C = 180°
35° + 65° + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180°
100° +{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 80°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 45° and {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 65°, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}C is
questions.explanation:
The sum of all the interior angles of a triangle equals 180°.
{tex}\angle {/tex}A + {tex}\angle {/tex}B +{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180°
45° + 65° + {tex}\angle {/tex}C=180°
110° + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180° 
{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180° - 110°
= 70°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}XYZ, {tex}\angle {/tex}X = 60o and {tex}\angle {/tex}Z = 65o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}Y is:
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of the triangle is 180°
X + Y + Z = 180°
Y = 180 - (65 + 60) = 55°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle {/tex}Q = 50o and {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 70o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}P is:
questions.explanation:
The sum of all the interior angles of a triangle equals 180°.
{tex}\angle {/tex}P + {tex}\angle {/tex}Q + {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P + 50° + 70° = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P + 120° = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P = 180° - 120°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P = 60°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle {/tex}P = 70o and {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 60o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}Q is
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of triangle is 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P +{tex}\angle {/tex}Q + {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}Q = 180 - (70 + 60) = 50°

questions.question: The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 17 cm long. If one of the remaining two sides is 8 cm in length, then the length of the other side is:
questions.explanation:
By pythagoras theorem,
Hypotenuse2 = sum of sqaures of other two sides
172 = 82 + x2
x2 = 225
x = 15cm

questions.question: The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 25 cm long. If one of the remaining two sides is 24 cm in length, then the length of the other side is:
questions.explanation:
As per Pythagoras theorem
Hypotenuse2 =  sum of squares of other two sides
Let the third side b x cm
So, 252 = 242 + x2 
x2 = 625 - 576
x2 = 49
x = 7 cm

questions.question: The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 26 cm long. If one of the remaining two sides is 10 cm in length, then the length of the other side is
questions.explanation:
As per Pythagoras theorem
Hypotenuse2 =  sum of squares of other two sides
Let the third side b x cm
262 = 102 + x2
x2 = 676 - 100
x2 = 576
x = 24 cm

questions.question: The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 10 cm long. If one of the remaining two sides is 8 cm in length, then the length of the other side is
questions.explanation:
As per Pythagoras theorem
Hypotenuse2 =  sum of squares of other two sides
let the third side b x cm
102 = 82 + x2
x2 = 100 - 64
x2 = 36
x = 6 cm

questions.question: The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 37 cm long. If one of the remaining two sides is 12 cm in length, then the length of the other side is
questions.explanation:
As per pythgoras theorem
Hypotenuse2 =  sum of squares of other two sides
Let the third side b x cm
372 = 122 + x2
x2 = 1369 - 144
x2 = 1225
x = 35 cm

questions.question: How many acute angles can a right-angled triangle have?
questions.explanation:
A right triangle can, and must, have exactly two acute angles.
To see this, just remember that the sum of the angles of a triangle must be 180°.  As a right triangle has one angle equal to 90°, this means the sum of the remaining two angles must be 180° - 90° = 90°.
Thus, both of the remaining two angles must have a measure less than 90° and therefore must be acute.

questions.question: Find the unknown length x in the adjoining figure.
questions.explanation:
As it is right-angled triangle,

By using pythgoras theorem,

x²=6²+8²

=36+64

=100

x=10

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 105o and {tex}\angle {/tex}Q = 40o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}P is:
questions.explanation:
Sum of all 3 angles of triangle is 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P +{tex}\angle {/tex}Q +{tex}\angle {/tex}R = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P = 180 - (105 + 40)
= 180 - 145
= 35°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 72o and {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 63o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}C is
questions.explanation:
Sum of all 3 angles of triangle is 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}A +{tex}\angle {/tex}B +{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180 - (72 + 63)
= 180 - 135
= 45°

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex}XYZ, {tex}\angle {/tex}X = 90o and {tex}\angle {/tex}Z = 48o, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}Y is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle {/tex}X +{tex}\angle {/tex}Y + {tex}\angle {/tex}Z = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}Y = 180 - (90 + 48)
= 180 - 138
= 42°

questions.question: One of the acute angle of a right triangle is 36o, then the other acute angle is:
Find the unknown length of x in the adjoining figure.
questions.explanation:
According to Pythagorean (or Pythagoras) Theorem
In Right angle triangle
(hypo)2 = (side)2 + (side)2
x2 = 32 + 42
x2 = 9 + 16
x2 = 25
x = {tex}\sqrt{25}{/tex}
x = 5

questions.question: Find the unknown length x in the below figure.
questions.explanation:
According to Pythagorean (or Pythagoras') Theorem
In Right angle triangle
(hypo)2 = (side)2 + (side)2
x2 = 152 + 82
x2 = 225 + 64
x2 = 289
x = {tex}\sqrt{289}{/tex}
x = 17 cm

questions.question: The acute angles of right triangle are in the ratio 2 : 1. Find the measure of each of these angles.
questions.explanation:
Let an angle be x
2x + x = 90
3x = 90
x = 30°
2x = 60°

questions.question: One of the angles of a triangle is 100° and the other two angles are equal. Find the measure of each of these equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of triangle = 180°
So 100 + 2x = 180
2x = 180 - 100 = 80
x = 40° each

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 110o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 25o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle.
Let the other angle be x
110 = 25 + x
x = 110 - 25
x = 85°

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 100o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 45o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle
let the other angle be x
so, 100 = 45 + x
x = 100 - 45
x = 55°

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 90o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 45o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle
let the other angle be x
so, 90 = 45 + x
x = 90 - 45
x = 45°

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 80o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 22o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle
Let the other angle be x
So, 80 = 22 + x
x = 80 - 22
x = 58°

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 85o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 30o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle
Let the other angle be x
So, 85 = 30 + x
x = 85 - 30
x = 55°

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 50o and 80o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle
let the exterior angle be x
x = 50 + 80
x = 130°

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 60o and 55o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to the exterior angle
Let the exterior angle be x
x = 60 + 55
x = 115°

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 40o and 30o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle.
let the exterior angle be x.
x = 30 + 40
x = 70°

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 50o and 50o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle.
Let the exterior angle be x.
x = 50 + 50
x = 100°

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 40o and 80o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle.
Let the exterior angle be x.
x = 40 + 80
x = 120°

questions.question: Find the value of x in the given figure.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to an exterior angle
so, 75 = 35 + x
x = 75 - 35
x = 40°

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is of measure 113o and one of its interior opposite angles is of measure 25o. Find the measure of the other interior opposite angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle.
Let the other angle be x.
So,113 = 25 + x
x = 113 - 25
x = 88°

questions.question: The two interior opposite angles of an exterior angle of a triangle are 59o and 51o. Find the measure of the exterior angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two opposite interior angle is equal to exterior angle.
Let the exterior angle be x
x = 51 + 59
x = 110°

questions.question: Two angles of a triangle are 30o and 80o. Find the third angle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of triangle is 180°
So, let the third angle be x
30 + 80 + x = 180
x = 180 - 110
x = 70°

questions.question: In the given figure, find {tex}\angle {/tex}P
questions.explanation:
The sum of all the interior angles of a triangle equals 180°.
{tex}\angle {/tex}P +{tex}\angle {/tex}Q +{tex}\angle {/tex}R = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P + 47° + 52° = 180°
{tex}\angle {/tex}P + 99° = 180
{tex}\angle {/tex} P = 180° - 99°​​​​​​​
{tex}\angle {/tex}P = 81°

questions.question: One of the angles of a triangle is 120o and the other two angles are equal. Find the measure of each of the equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of triangle is 180°
So, 120 + 2x = 180
2x = 180 - 120 = 60
x = 30° each

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 5:4:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Let an angle be x
So, 5x + 4x + x = 180°
10x = 180°
x = 18°
So,
5x = 90°
4x = 72°

questions.question: Lengths of sides of a triangle are 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm. The triangle is
questions.explanation:
Pythagorean Theorem Converse: If the square of one side of a triangle is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides, then the triangle is a right triangle.
(3)2 + (4)2 = 9 + 16 = 25
(5)2 = 25
So (3)2 + (4)2 = (5)2
So given triangle is right angled triangle.

questions.question: Find the value of x in the given figure:-
questions.explanation:
If the given triangle is ABC.

According to Pythagorean (or Pythagoras') Theorem
In Right angle triangle ADC
(hypo)2 = (side)2 + (side)2
372 = 122 + (DC)2
1369  = 144 +  (DC)2
(DC)2 = 1369 - 144
(DC)2  = 1225
DC = {tex}\sqrt{1225}{/tex}
DC = 35
Same as In Right angle triangle ADB
BD = 35
So x = BD + DC = 35 + 35 = 70

questions.question: A 15 m long ladder reached a window 12 m high from the ground on placing it against a wall at a distance a. Find the distance of the foot of the ladder from the wall.
questions.explanation:
Let C be the position of the window on the wall and AC be the ladder
So, BC = 12 m and AC = 15 m
Distance of the foot of the ladder from the wall = AB
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, Angle B = 90°
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AC2 = AB2 + BC2 [By Pythagoras property]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 152 = AB2 + 122
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 225 = AB2 + 144
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB2 = 225 – 144
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB2 = 81
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = {tex}\sqrt{81}{/tex} = 9 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The distance of the foot of the ladder from the wall is 9 m.

questions.question: The top of a broken tree touches the ground at a distance of 12 m from its base. If the tree is broken at a height of 5 m from the ground then the actual height of the tree is
questions.explanation:
Let DCB represent the tree before it broken at the point C. In the given figure, BC represents the unbroken part of the tree. Point C represents the point where the tree broke and CA represents the broken part of the tree. Triangle ABC, thus formed, is right-angled at B.

Applying Pythagoras theorem in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC,
AC2 = BC2 + AB2
AC2 = (5 m)2 + (12 m)2
AC2 = 25 m2 + 144 m2 = 169 m2
AC = 13 m
Thus, original height of the tree = AC + CB = 13 m + 5 m = 18 m

questions.question: Find the perimeter of the rectangle whose length is 40 cm and a diagonal is 41 cm.
questions.explanation:
Breadth of the rectangle can be calculated by using Pythagoras rule because length, breadth and diagonal would make a right-angled triangle.
So, h2 = l2 + b2
Or, 412 = 402 + b2
Or, 1681 = 1600 + b2
Or, b2 = 1681 - 1600 = 81
Or, b2 = 9 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
Or, b = 9 cm
Perimeter can be calculated as follows:
Perimeter = 2(length + breadth)
= 2(40 + 9) = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 49 = 98 cm

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus measure 16 cm and 30 cm. Find its perimeter.
questions.explanation:
Halves of diagonals of a rhombus make the legs of a right angle triangle while hypotenuse is made by a side of the rhombus. So, side of the rhombus can be calculated by using Pythagoras rule;
We know; h2 = l2 + b2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  h2 = 82 + 152 = 64 + 225 = 289
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h2 = 17 {tex}\times{/tex} 17
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} h = 17 cm
Hence, Perimeter = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} side = 17 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 68 cm

questions.question: The triangle whose lengths of sides are 8 cm, 15 cm, 17 cm is a ____.
questions.explanation:
(15)2 + (8)2 = 225 + 64 = 289
(17)2 = 289
So (15)2 + (8)2 = (17)2
If the square of one side of a triangle is equal to the sum of the squares of the other two sides, then the triangle is a right triangle.
So given triangle is right angle triangle.

questions.question: The triangle whose lengths of sides are 7 cm, 24 cm, 25 cm is a _________.
questions.explanation:
As per pythagorus theorem, 
base2 + altitude2 = hypotenuse2
so, here, 
72 + 242 = 252

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 6:3:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all three angles of triangle is 180°
Thus, 6x + 3x + x = 180°
10x = 180°
x = 18°
So, 6x = 108°
3x = 54°

questions.question: One of the angles of a triangle is 150o and the other two angles are equal. Find the measure of each of the equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Sum of all angles of triangle is 180°
so, 150 + x + x = 180°
2x = 180 - 150 = 30°
x = 15°

As there are five congruence criteria:

  2. Angle Side Angle
  3. Side side side

Under the correspondence: ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RQP
Then, 
Then, {tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}\angle {/tex}R

Then {tex}\angle {/tex}B ={tex}\angle {/tex}Q

questions.question: {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR are congruent under the correspondence: ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RPQ, then the part of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC that corresponds to PQ is:

QR = CB

questions.question: What is the side included between the angles A and B in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC?
questions.explanation:
The vertex of the angle is the point where the two sides of the angle meet. These would be the sides that include the angle.
So when the vertex A and vertex B meets the side AB is formed it means there is side AB between the angle A and angle B.

questions.question: In the below quadrilateral ABCD, AD = BC and {tex}\angle {/tex}DAB = {tex}\angle {/tex}CBA. If {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABD {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}BAC. The relation between {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD and {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC is
questions.explanation: When the two triangles are congruent to each other then all sides and the angles are equal so that {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC

questions.question: In the quadrilateral ABCD, AC = AD and AB bisect {tex}\angle {/tex}A and {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABD. The relation between BC and BD is
questions.explanation: When {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABD by any congruency like in this case it is SAS, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure. So that BC = BD

questions.question: In the below figure, AC and BD are equal perpendiculars to line segment AB. If {tex}\triangle {/tex}BOC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}AOD, then the relation between OC and OD is
questions.explanation: When {tex}\triangle {/tex}BOC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}AOD by any congruency like in this case it is AAS, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure. So that OC = OD

questions.question: In the below figure, AB = AC and BE = CD. If {tex}\triangle {/tex}ACD {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABE then AD =
questions.explanation: When  {tex}\triangle {/tex}ACD {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABE by any congruency, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure. So that AD = AE

questions.question: {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is right triangle in which {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 90o and AB = AC. The values of {tex}\angle {/tex}B and {tex}\angle {/tex}C will be:
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree.
So {tex}\angle {/tex}A + {tex}\angle {/tex}B + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180
90 + {tex}\angle {/tex}B + {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 180 [As side AB = AC]
2 {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 180 - 90
{tex}\angle {/tex}B = 45 =  {tex}\angle {/tex}C

questions.question: The measure of each angle of an equilateral triangle is:
questions.explanation:
Let ABC is an equilateral triangle it means all the sides and angles are same.
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree.
So {tex}\angle {/tex}A + {tex}\angle {/tex}B + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180
3 {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 180 [ All angles are equal ]
{tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}\angle {/tex}B ={tex}\angle {/tex}C = 60

questions.question: If the vertical angle of a isosceles triangle is 40o, then measure of other two angles will be:
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree.
So A + B + C = 180
40 + B + B = 180 [As ABC is an isoscles triangle then side AB = AC]
2 B = 180 - 40
B = 70 =  C

questions.question: If {tex}\angle {/tex}A, {tex}\angle {/tex}B and {tex}\angle {/tex}C of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC are equal, then triangle is:
questions.explanation: Equilateral triangle is triangle whose all the sides and angles are equal which is 60 degree in case of an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: Which one of the following is the criteria of congruency?
questions.explanation: As there are six criteria of congruency: SSS, SAS, ASA, RHS, AAS and AAA

questions.question: By which congruence rule following triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: Because all the sides of both the triangle are equal with each other.

BC = AD

questions.question: Which is the longest side in the triangle ABC right angled at B?
questions.explanation: Because the side opposite to the angle of 90 degree is hypotenus which is the longest side of the triangle.

questions.question: Which is the longest side of a right triangle?
questions.explanation: The side opposite to the angle of 90 degrees is the longest side (hypotenuse).

questions.question: What is the angle included between the sides PN and PM of {tex}\triangle {/tex}MNP?
questions.explanation: As the common angle is {tex}\angle {/tex}P between PN and PM.

questions.question: In triangles ABC and PQR, AB = 3.5 cm, BC = 7.1 cm, AC = 5 cm, PQ = 7.1 cm, QR = 5 cm and PR = 3.5 cm, then which of the following is true?
questions.explanation:
As BC = PQ
AB = RP
AC = QR

questions.question: A triangle in which all three sides are of equal lengths is called _________.
questions.explanation: Equilateral triangle is a triangle whose all the sides and angles are equal which is 60 degree in case of an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: In triangles ABC and DEF, AB = 7 cm, BC = 5 cm, {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 50o, DE = 5 cm, EF = 7 cm, {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 50o. By which congruence rule the triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: In the given figure, two sides and one angle are equal, hence it's SAS (Side Angle Side) type of congruency.

questions.question: In the below figure, AB = AC and AD is the bisector of {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC, then the relation between {tex}\angle {/tex}B and {tex}\angle {/tex}C is
questions.explanation: As the side, AB = AC, the two equal side and their two opposite angle are also equal with each other.

QT = QS

questions.question: A triangle has ________ vertices, three sides and three angles.
questions.explanation: A Triangle is closed figure which is made up of three lines and three angles.

questions.question: {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR are congruent under the correspondence ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RQP. Write the parts of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC that correspond to RQ.
questions.explanation:
As per the congruency of ABC and RQP.
RQ = AB
QP = BC
RP = AC

questions.question: Which angle is included between the sides DE and EF of {tex}\triangle {/tex}DEF?
questions.explanation: As E is the common between DE and EF so the {tex}\angle {/tex}E is the only angle between angle D and F.

questions.question: By applying SAS congruence rule, you want to establish that {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}FED. It is given that PQ = FE and RP = DF. What additional information is needed to establish the congruence?
questions.explanation:
As per SAS congruency in {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}FED. As given that PQ = FE and RP = DF.
Then {tex}\angle {/tex}P = {tex}\angle {/tex}F
{tex}\angle {/tex}Q = {tex}\angle {/tex}E
{tex}\angle {/tex}R = {tex}\angle {/tex}D

questions.question:
Which congruence criterion do you use in the following?
Given: AC = DF, AB = DE, BC = EF. So, {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex}\cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}DEF
questions.explanation: As all the sides of both the triangle are equal to each other.

questions.question: If two line segments have the _________ length, they are congruent.
questions.explanation: As when two line segments or sides are equal in length, then we can say that they are congruent.

questions.question: If two triangles are congruent, then their _________ parts (i.e., angles and sides) that match one another are equal.
questions.explanation: If two triangles are congruent, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure.

questions.question: Which of the following examines the congruence of plane figures?
questions.explanation: Superposition method

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of A.S.A. criterion of congruency for two triangles ∠ADB and ∠DEF?
questions.explanation: BC=EF, ∠B=∠E and ∠C=∠F

questions.question: The sum of the measures of the three angles of a triangle is ________.
questions.explanation: Yes, the sum of the measures of the three angles of a triangle should be 180 degrees, if it is more than or less than the 180 degrees then it is not a triangle.

questions.question: In an equilateral triangle each angle has measure ________.
questions.explanation:
Equilateral triangle is trangle whose all the sides and angles are equal which is 60 degree in case of equilateral triangle.
x + x + x = 180
3x = 180
x = 60

questions.question: A triangle in which two sides are of equal lengths is called an _________.
questions.explanation:
Given: ABC is an isosceles triangle angle B = angle C
To Prove: AB = AC
Construction: Draw a bisector of angle A intersecting BC at D
Proof:  In triangle ABD and ACD
angle B = angle C (Given)
AD = AD (Common)
angle BAD = angle CAD (By construction)
triangle ABD and ACD are congruent (By ASA Congruency)
AB = AC (By CPCT)

questions.question: {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR are congruent under the correspondence ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RQP. Write the parts of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC that correspond to PQ.
questions.explanation:
Under the congruency of ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RQP
AB = RQ
BC = QP 
AC = RP

questions.question: How many medians can a triangle have?
questions.explanation: In geometry, a median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposing side. Every triangle has exactly three medians, one from each vertex, and they all intersect each other at the triangle's centroid.

questions.question: If the Pythagoras property holds for some triangle, will the triangle be:
questions.explanation:
Pythagoras' Theorem applies to right triangles and states (in word form):
"The square of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the base and the perpendicular."

questions.question: A ________ connects a vertex of a triangle to the mid-point of the opposite side.
questions.explanation: In geometry, a median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposing side. Every triangle has exactly three medians, one from each vertex, and they all intersect each other at the triangle's centroid.

questions.question: Which angle is included between the sides AB and AC of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC?
questions.explanation: As A is the common between AB and AC so the A is the only angle between angle B and C.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle ________ sides have same length.
questions.explanation:
Given: ABC is an isosceles triangle angle B = angle C
To Prove: AB = AC
Construction: Draw a bisector of angle A intersecting BC at D
Proof:  In triangle ABD and ACD
angle B = angle C (Given)
AD = AD (Common)
angle BAD = angle CAD (By construction)
triangle ABD and ACD are congrue0nt (By ASA Congruency)
AB = AC (By CPCT)

questions.question: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the ________.
questions.explanation:
The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side, otherwise the triangle not possible.
For Example: 3cm + 6cm > 7cm

questions.question: Which of the following criterion does not exist?
questions.explanation: A.A.A. criterion

questions.question: The lengths of two sides of a triangle are 12 cm and 15 cm. Between what two measures should the length of the third side fall?

options.html: greater than 2 and less than 30 cm
greater than 9 and less than 27 cm
greater than 2 and less than 27 cm
greater than 9 and less than 30 cm

questions.explanation: The sum of the lengths of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side. Here, the third side will be lesser than the sum (12 + 15) = 27 cm and more than the difference of (15 - 12) = 3 cm. So it means the third side fall between 2 cm to 27 cm ie. it should be more than 2cm and less than the 27 cm.

questions.question: An ________ is formed when lines or line segments meet.
questions.explanation: An angle is a combination of two rays (half-lines) with a common endpoint.

questions.question: Determine whether the triangle whose lengths of sides are 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm is a ________.
questions.explanation: Because If a triangle is​ a right triangle with sides a < b < c, then the Pythagorean formula is true of the sides. Here, 3 cm < 4 cm < 5 cm.

questions.question: A median wholly lie in the ________ of the triangle.
questions.explanation: In geometry, a median of a triangle is a line segment joining a vertex to the midpoint of the opposing side. Every triangle has exactly three medians, one from each vertex, and they all intersect each other at the triangle's centroid.

questions.question: The sum of an exterior angle of a triangle and its adjacent interior angle is ________.
questions.explanation:
Because an exterior angle of a triangle and its adjacent interior angle make linear pair which is 180 degree. So, the exterior angle is a supplement to the adjoining interior angle.
For Example: angle ABC is 70 degree and its exterior angle should be 110 degree so that (70 + 110) = 180 degree.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle base angles opposite to the equal sides are ________.
questions.explanation:
Given: ABC is an isosceles triangle AB = AC
To Prove: angle B = angle C
Construction: Draw a bisector of angle A intersecting BC at D
Proof:  In triangle ABD and ACD
AB = AC (Given)
AD = AD (Common)
angle BAD = angle CAD (By construction)
triangle ABD and ACD are congruent (By SAS Congruency)
angle B = angle C (By CPCT)

questions.question: The side opposite to the right angle is called the ________ of the right-angled triangle.
questions.explanation:
As the side opposite to the right angle is known as hypotenuse and it is the largest side of the right-angled triangle.
In a right triangle (one where one interior angle is 90°), the longest side is called the hypotenuse. It is always the side opposite the 90° angle.
Pythagoras' Theorem applies to right triangles and states (in word form):
The square on the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares on the other two sides.

questions.question:
Which congruence criterion do you use in the following?
Given: ZX = RP, RQ = ZY, ∠PRQ = ∠XZY. So, {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}XYZ
questions.explanation: As there are two sides and one angle of a triangle is equal to another triangle, so SAS congruency is applicable.

questions.question: If two line segments are ________, they have the same length.
questions.explanation: Yes, when the two line segment are equal in length then we can say that they are congruent.

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 4:5:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
As we know the sum of the measures of the three angles of a triangle is 180 degree
Let, 4x + 5x + x = 180
10x = 180
x = 18
4x = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 =72, 5x = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 90 and x = 18

questions.question: Which congruence rule show the following triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: In two right-angled triangles, if the length of the hypotenuse and one side of one triangle is equal to the length of the hypotenuse and one side of the other triangle, then the two triangles are congruent. Here, all the conditions of RHS congruency are satisfied.

questions.question: The two triangles, the three angles of one triangle are correspondingly equal to three angles of another triangle. Which of the following is a correct statement?

options.html: The two triangles are congruent by A.A.A. congruency criterion.
One triangle is an enlarged copy of other.
The two triangles are necessarily congruent.

questions.explanation: If the three angles of a triangle are congruent to corresponding angles of the other, it is an enlarged copy of the triangle.

questions.question: In the below figure, BD and CE are altitudes of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC such that BD = CE then by which congruence rule {tex}\triangle {/tex}CBD {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}BCE?
questions.explanation:
In two right-angled triangles, if the length of the hypotenuse and one side of one triangle is equal to the length of the hypotenuse and one side of the other triangle, then the two triangles are congruent. Here all the conditions of RHS are satisfied.
In triangle BDC and CEB
AS angle E and D are 90 degrees
one side is common in both the triangle
and the hypotenuse BD = CE is given

questions.question: It is to be established by RHS congruence rule that {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}RPQ. What additional information is needed, if it is given that {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}P = 90o and AB = RP?
questions.explanation:
If it is given that {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}P = 90o and AB = RP and  RHS congruence rule that {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}RPQ, then
BC = PQ
AC = RQ

questions.question: If ABC and DEF are congruent triangles such that {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 47{tex}^\circ {/tex} and {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 83{tex}^\circ {/tex}, then {tex}\angle {/tex}C =
questions.explanation:
As we know the sum of the measures of the three angles of a triangle is 180 degree.
{tex}\angle {/tex}A + {tex}\angle {/tex}B + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180
47 + {tex}\angle {/tex}E + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180
47 + 83 + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180
{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180 - 130 = 50

questions.question: In congruent triangles ABC and DEF, {tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 40o, and {tex}\angle {/tex}F = 65o, then {tex}\angle {/tex}B =
questions.explanation:
As per congruency of triangles ABC and DEF,
{tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 40o 
{tex}\angle {/tex}C = {tex}\angle {/tex}F = 65o 
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree 
So A + B + C = 180
B = 180 - 40 - 65 = 75o
{tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 75o

questions.question: In the below figure, if EF = QR then the congruence rule used for the congruency of the given triangles is
questions.explanation:
Angle E = angle Q = 80o
Angle F = angle R = 80o
EF = QR

questions.question: In the below figure, by which congruence rule the following triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation:
As in the above two triangles, 

AC = AC

angle B = angle D = 90

hypotenuse, AB = AD with same lenght.

questions.question: In triangles ABC and PQR, {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 90o, AC = 8 cm, AB = 3 cm, {tex}\angle {/tex}P = 90o, PR = 3 cm, QR = 8 cm. By which congruence rule the triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: In two right-angled triangles, if the length of the hypotenuse and one side of one triangle is equal to the length of the hypotenuse and one side of the other triangle, then the two triangles are congruent. Here all the conditions of RHS are satisfied.

questions.question: The hypotenuse of a right triangle is 37 cm long. If one of the remaining two sides is 12 cm in length, then the length of the other side is
questions.explanation:
As per the pythagorous,  
H2 = P2 + B2
372 = 122 + B2
B2 = 1369 - 144 = 1225
B = 35

questions.question: How many acute angles can a right triangle have?
questions.explanation: Two angles are acute because one angle is 90 degree and two must be less than 90 degrees as the angle sum property is 180 so the sum of other two angles should be (180 - 90) = 90 degrees.

questions.question: If two triangles are ________, then their corresponding parts (i.e., angles and sides) that match one another are equal.
questions.explanation: If two triangles are congruent it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure.

questions.question: In triangles DEF and PQR, {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 80o, {tex}\angle {/tex}F = 30o, EF = 5 cm, {tex}\angle {/tex}P = 80o, PQ = 5 cm, {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 30o. By which congruence rule the triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: In two triangles, if the one side and two angles of one triangle is equal to the other triangle, it means both triangles are congruent with ASA congruency. Here all the conditions of ASA are satisfied.

questions.question: {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR are congruent under the correspondence ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RQP. Write the parts of ΔABC that correspond to RP.
questions.explanation: As per the congruency ABC {tex} \leftrightarrow {/tex} RQP, AB = RQ; BC = QP and AC = RQ

questions.question: Which angle is included between the sides QR and PR of {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR?
questions.explanation: Angle R is the correct answer as the vertex R is common in both the sides PR and QR.

questions.question: One of the acute angle of a right triangle is 36o, then the other acute angle is:
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree.
Other angle of the right angle triangle is 180 - 90 - 36 = 54 degree.

questions.question: What is the side included between the angles M and N of {tex}\triangle {/tex}MNP?
questions.explanation: MN is the only side between angle M and N as the vertex of M and N draw the line MN.

questions.question:
Which congruence criterion do you use in the following?
Given: ∠MLN = ∠FGH, ∠NML = ∠GFH, ML = FG. So, {tex}\triangle {/tex}LMN {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}GFH
questions.explanation: In two triangles, if the one side and two angles of one triangle is equal to the other triangle, it means both triangles are congruent with ASA congruency. Here all the conditions of ASA are satisfied.

questions.question: The relation of two objects being congruent is called ________.
questions.explanation: Congruence is a relationship between two images indicating that the image has the same length and angles like another image.

questions.question: In the given figure, by which congruence rule the following triangles are congruent?
questions.explanation: It is an ASA congruency, as there are two angles ie angle A and C of both the triangle are equal with each other and side AC is common.

questions.question:

questions.explanation: In two right-angled triangles, if the length of the hypotenuse and one side of one triangle is equal to the length of the hypotenuse and one side of the other triangle, then the two triangles are congruent. Here all the conditions of RHS are satisfied.

questions.question: You want to establish {tex}\triangle {/tex}DEF {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}MNP, using the ASA congruence rule. questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 70{tex}^\circ {/tex}; the measure of the other angle is _________.
questions.explanation: The measure of the other angle is 70o. Among two congruent angles, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure.

questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 55o; the measure of the other angle is ________.
questions.explanation: The measure of the other angle is 55o, Among two congruent angles, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure.

questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 130o; the measure of the other angle is ________.
questions.explanation: The measure of the other angle is 130o. Among two congruent angles, it means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure.

questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 115o; the measure of the other angle is ________.

questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 80o; the measure of the other angle is ___________.

questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 100o; the measure of the other angle is ________.

questions.question: Among two congruent angles, one has a measure of 120o; the measure of the other angle is ________.
questions.explanation: The measure of the other angle is 120o, because the congruency means that every corresponding side has the same length, and every corresponding angle has the same measure.
