questions.question: One of the angles of a triangle is 120o and the other two angles are equal. Find the measure of each of the equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree
So A + B + C = 180
120 + B+ B = 180 [As two sides are equal]|
B = {tex}\frac{60}{2}{/tex} = 30o

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 5:4:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree
So A + B + C = 180
5x + 4x + x = 180
x = 18
A = 5 {tex}\times{/tex}18 = 90
B = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 72
C = 18

questions.question: By applying ASA congruence rule, it is to be established that {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}QRP and it is given that BC = RP. What additional information is needed to establish the congruence?
questions.explanation:
By applying ASA congruence rule, it is to be established that {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}QRP
{tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}\angle {/tex}Q,  {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}R and {tex}\angle {/tex}C = {tex}\angle {/tex}P
BC = RP (Given)

questions.question: If {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR are to be congruent, name one additional pair of corresponding parts.
questions.explanation:
If {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}QPR are to be congruent, then
angle C = angle R
angle B = angle Q = 90o
BC = QR

questions.question: Find the value of x and y in the below figure.
questions.explanation:
x = 60o (Vertically opposite angles)
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree
30o + x+ y = 180o
30o + 60o + y = 180o
Y = 180o - 90o = 90

questions.question: The triangle whose lengths of sides are 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm is a ________.
questions.explanation: If a triangle is​ a right triangle with sides a < b < c, then the Pythagorean formula is true of the sides. Here, the triangle whose lengths of sides are 3 cm, 4 cm, 5 cm means the condition of sides a < b < c then it is proved that it is a right-angled triangle.

questions.question: Find the value of x in the below figure.
questions.explanation:
Pythagoras' Theorem applies to right triangles and states (in word form) - "The square on the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares on the other two sides."
H2 = P2 + B2
P2 = 372 - 122 = 1225
P = 35
For both triangle Perpendicular = 35 + 35 = 70

questions.question: A 15 m long ladder reached a window 12 m high from the ground on placing it against a wall at a distance a. Find the distance of the foot of the ladder from the wall.
questions.explanation:
Using the Pythagorean Theorem, we can find the distance of foot of laddder from ground.
H2 = P2 + B2
152 = 122 + B2
B2 =  225 - 144 = 81
B = 9cm

questions.question: A tree is broken at a height of 5 m from the ground and its top touches the ground at a distance of 12 m from the base of the tree. Find the original height of the tree.
questions.explanation:
If you draw the diagram you have a right triangle with 6m going up and 8m at the base.
Using the Pythagorean Theorem, we can find the length of the fallen part of the pole.
H2 = P2 + B2 
H2 = 122 + 52 = 144 + 25 = 169
H = 13
Orignal height of tree = 13 + 5 = 18cm

questions.question: Find the perimeter of the rectangle whose length is 40 cm and a diagonal is 41 cm.
questions.explanation:
41cm is the diagonal and 40cm is the length.
hyp2 = length2 + breadth2
412 = 402 + breadth2
1681 = 1600 + breadth2
1681 - 1600 = breadth2
breadth2 = 81
breadth = {tex}\sqrt{81}{/tex}
= 9cm
Perimeter = 2(l + b)
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} (40 + 9)
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 49
= 98cm

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus measure 16 cm and 30 cm. Find its perimeter.
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a rhombus, AC and BD are two diagonal intersect at point M.
AC =  16cm BD = 30 cm
AM = {tex}\frac{16}{2}{/tex} = 8cm and DM = {tex}\frac{30}{2}{/tex} = 15cm
Diagonals of the perimeter are perpendicular to each other. So, we can use the Pythagorean theorem to find the side length of the rhombus.
IN triangle ADM
82 + 152 = AD2
64 + 225 = 289 = AD2
AD Side length(s) = 17 and Perimeter of rhombus = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 17 = 68 cm

questions.question: The triangle whose lengths of sides are 8 cm, 15 cm, 17 cm is a ________.
questions.explanation:
Pythagoras' Theorem applies to right triangles and states (in word form):
"The square of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the base and the perpendicular." We know hypotenuse is the longest side.
Here, H2 ​​​​​​= P2 ​​​​​+ B2
172 = 152 + 82
289 = 225 + 64
289 = 289
As L.H.S. = R.H.S. Therefore triangle is right angled.

questions.question: The triangle whose lengths of sides are 7 cm, 24 cm, 25 cm is a ________.
questions.explanation:
Pythagoras' Theorem applies to right triangles and states (in word form):
"The square of the hypotenuse is equal to the sum of the squares of the base and the perpendicular." We know hypotenuse is the longest side.
Here, H2​​​​​​ = P2 + B2
252 = 242 + 72
625 = 576 + 49
625 = 625
As L.H.S. = R.H.S. Therefore, the triangle is right angled.

questions.question: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio 6:3:1. Find all the angles of the triangle.
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree
So A + B + C = 180
6x + 3x + x = 180
10x = 180
x =18
A = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 108
B = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 18 = 54
C = 18

questions.question: One of the angles of a triangle is 150o and the other two angles are equal. Find the measure of each of the equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Angle sum property of a triangle is 180 degree
So A + B + C = 180
150 + B + B = 180 [As side AB = AC]
2B = 180 -150 = 30
B = 15o
C =  15o

tests.name: Comparing Quantities Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 7 Comparing Quantities CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Find the ratio of 3 km to 300 m.
questions.explanation:
1 km = 1000m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 3 km = 3000 m
{tex}{3000 \over 300} {/tex} = 10 : 1
So, it is 10 : 1

questions.question: A map is given with a scale of 2 cm = 1000 km. What is the actual distance between the two places in kms, if the distance in the map is 2.5 cm?
questions.explanation:
Given that 2cm = 1000km
2cm {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 1000km
2.5cm {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} x km
By unitary method,
x = {tex}{2.5 \times 1000 \over 2}{/tex} = 1250 km

questions.question: 6 bowls cost ₹90. What would be the cost of 10 such bowls?
questions.explanation:
Given cost of 6 bowls = ₹90
Cost of 1 bowl = {tex} {90 \over 6} {/tex} = ₹15
Therefore, cost of 10 such bowls = 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = ₹150

questions.question: The car that I own can go 150 km with 25 litres of petrol. How far can it go with 30 litres of petrol?
questions.explanation:
The car with 25 litres of petrol can go = 150 km
Then, with 1 litre of petrol can go = {tex}\frac{150}{25}{/tex} = 6
With 30 litres of petrol can go = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 180 km

questions.question: The ratio of 90 cm to 1.5 m is
questions.explanation:
1m = 100 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 1.5 m = 150 cm
x = {tex}\frac{90}{150}= \frac{3}{ 5}{/tex}
The ratio is 3 : 5

questions.question: A picnic is being planned in a school for Class VII. Girls are 60% of the total number of students and are 18 in number. The ratio of the number of girls to the number of boys in the class is:
questions.explanation:
Let the total number of students be x.
There are 60 girls out of 100 students be x.
60% of x is girls. Therefore, 60% of x = 18
So, 18 girls are out of how many students?
{tex}{60\over 100} × x = 18 {/tex}
x = {tex}{18 \times100\over 60}{/tex} = 30
The total number of students are: 30
Number of boys = 30 - 18 = 12
The ratio is = {tex}\frac{18}{12}{/tex} = 3:2

questions.question: Find the ratio of Speed of a cycle 15 km per hour to the speed of scooter 30 km per hour.
questions.explanation: ratio = {tex}{15kmph\over 30kmph} {/tex} = 1:2

questions.question: 20% of 700 m is
questions.explanation:
20% of 700 m
= {tex}\left(\frac{20}{100} \times 700\right){/tex}
= 140 m

questions.question: Find the ratio of 50 paise to ₹5.
questions.explanation:
1 rupee = 100 paise
5 rupees = 500 paise
Ratio : {tex}{50 \over 500} {/tex} = 1:10

questions.question: 72% of 25 students are good in Hindi, how many are not good in Hindi?
questions.explanation:
72% of 25 = {tex}\frac{72}{100} \times 25{/tex} = 18
Number of students not good in hindi = 25 - 18 = 7

questions.question: In a computer lab, there are 3 computers for every 6 students. How many computers will be needed for 24 students?
questions.explanation:
3 computers for every 6 students.
That means 1 computer for 2 students.
Then, for 24 students need computers = {tex}\frac{24}{2}{/tex} = 12

questions.question: Out of 32 students, 8 are absent. What percent of the students are present?
questions.explanation:
% of students absent = {tex}\frac{8}{32} \times 100{/tex} = 25%
% of students present = 100 - 25 = 75%

questions.question: There are 25 radios, 16 of them are out of order. What percent of radios are out of order?
questions.explanation:
Total number of radios = 25.
Number of radios out of order = 16
% of the radios of order = {tex}\frac{16}{25} \times 100{/tex} = 64%

questions.question: A shop has 500 parts, out of which 5 are defective. What percent are not defective?
questions.explanation:
% of defective parts = {tex}\frac{5}{500} \times 100{/tex} = 1
% of non-defective parts = 100 - 1 = 99

questions.question: There are 120 voters, 90 of them voted yes. What percent voted yes?
questions.explanation: % of voters who voted yes = {tex}\frac{90}{120} \times 100{/tex} = 75%

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A survey of 40 children showed that 25% liked playing football. How many children not liked playing football?
questions.explanation:
25% of 40 students = {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times 40{/tex} = 10
number of students who like football = 10
{tex}\therefore{/tex} number of studetns who don't like football = 40 - 10 = 30

questions.question: 8% children of a class of 25 like getting wet in the rain. How many children do not like getting wet in the rain.
questions.explanation:
8% 25 children = {tex}\frac{8}{100} \times 25{/tex} = 2: number of students who like rain.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} number of students who do not like rain = 25 - 2 = 23

questions.question: Rahul bought a sweater and saved ₹20 when a discount of 25% was given. What was the price of the sweater before the discount?
questions.explanation:
Let, actual cost price = ₹100
Discount of  25% = {tex}\frac{100 \times 25}{100}{/tex}= 25
Then, Amount is = 100 - 25 = 75 ₹.
₹25 is saved when the amount is = ₹100
₹1 is saved when the amount is = {tex}\frac{100}{25}{/tex}
₹20 is saved when the amount is = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = ₹80.

questions.question: Out of 15,000 voters in a constituency, 60% voted. Find the number of voters who did not vote.
questions.explanation:
Number of voters who voted = {tex}\frac{60}{100} \times 15000{/tex} = 9000 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} number of voters who did not vote = 15000 - 9000 = 6000

questions.question: Meeta saves ₹400 from her salary. If this is 10% of her salary. What is her salary?
questions.explanation:
Let salary be ₹x
10% of salary = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times x{/tex} = 400
Salary x = {tex}\frac{400 \times 100}{10}{/tex} = 4000

questions.question: A local cricket team played 20 matches in one season. It won 25% of them. How many matches did they lose?
questions.explanation:
Total number of matches played = 20 
25% of 20 matches = {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times 20{/tex} = 5
So, number of matches won = 5
{tex}\therefore{/tex} number of matches lost = 20 - 5 = 15

questions.question: A school team won 6 games this year against 4 games won last year. What is the percent increase?
questions.explanation:
Total increase = 6 - 4 = 2
percent increase = {tex}\frac{2}{4} \times 100{/tex} = 50%

questions.question: The number of illiterate persons in a country decreased from 150 lakhs to 100 lakhs in 10 years. What is the percentage of decrease?
questions.explanation:
net decrease = 150 lakhs - 100 lakhs = 50 lakhs
percentage decrease = {tex}\frac{50}{150} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}33 \frac{1}{3} \%{/tex}

questions.question: Cost of an item is ₹50. It was sold with a profit of 12%. Find the selling price.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of an item = ₹50
Selling price = {tex} {{ cost \ price \times (100+ profit\%)} \over 100}{/tex} 
Hence, Selling price with 12% profit = {tex}{{ 50 \times (100+ 12)} \over 100} {/tex} 
Selling price = {tex}{50\times 112 \over 100}{/tex} = ₹56

questions.question: How much will an item cost if 10% discount is given on the marked price ₹100?
questions.explanation:
Marked price = 100
%Discount = 10
Discount amount = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times 100{/tex} = 10
New price = 100 - 10 = 90

questions.question: A football team won 10 matches out of the total number of matches they played. If their win percentage was 40, then how many matches did they play in all?
questions.explanation:
Number of matches won = 10
Let total number of matches played be x.
Win percentage = 40%
Win percentage = {tex}\frac{\text {number of matches won}}{\text {total number of matches played}} \times 100{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 40 = {tex}\frac{10}{x} \times 100{/tex} 
x = {tex}\frac{10}{40} \times 100{/tex} = 25

questions.question: If Chameli had ₹600 left after spending 75% of her money, how much did she have in the beginning?
questions.explanation:
Let total amount be x.
75% of x is spent
So, 25% of x = ₹600
{tex}\frac{25}{100} \times x{/tex} = 600
x = {tex}\frac{600 \times 100}{25}{/tex} = 2400

questions.question: The price of a scooter was ₹34,000 last year. It has increased by 20% this year. What is the price now?
questions.explanation:
Price of a scooter = 34000
% Increase = 20%
Increase in amount = {tex}\frac{20}{100} \times 34000{/tex} = 6800
Total price = 34000 + 6800 = ₹40800

questions.question: The price of a scooter was ₹34,000 last year. It has decreased by 5% this year. What is the price now?
questions.explanation:
Price of a scooter = 34000
% Decrease = 5%
Increase in amount = {tex}\frac{5}{100} \times 34000{/tex} = 1700
Total price = 34000 - 1700 = ₹32,300

questions.question: Mohit bought a CD for ₹750 and sold it ₹875. Find his gain or loss percent.
questions.explanation:
Cost Price = ₹750
Selling price = ₹875
Gain = SP - CP = 875 - 750 = 125
Gain % = {tex}\frac{\operatorname{Gain}}{C P} \times 100{/tex} 
Gain % = {tex}\frac{125}{750} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}16 \frac{2}{3} \%{/tex}

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Rahul purchased a table for ₹1260 and due to some scratches on its top he had to sell it for ₹1197. Find his loss or gain percent.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of a scooter = 1260
Selling Price of a scooter
SP < CP
So, loss.
Loss = CP - SP = 1260 - 1197 = ₹63
Loss percent = {tex}\frac{\operatorname{loss}}{C P} \times 100=\frac{63}{1260} \times 100{/tex} = 5%

questions.question: Raghu bought an almirah for ₹6250 and spent ₹375 on its repairs. Then he sold it for ₹6890. Find his gain or loss percent.
questions.explanation:
Cost price = ₹6250
Repair cost = ₹375
Total CP = ₹6625
SP = ₹6890
SP > CP. SO Gain. Gain = SP - CP = 265
Gain % = {tex}\frac{\operatorname{Gain}}{C P} \times 100=\frac{265}{6625} \times 100{/tex} = 4%

questions.question: A vendor bought oranges at 20 for ₹56 and sold them at ₹35 per dozen. Find his gain or loss percent.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of 20 oranges = ₹56
Cost price of 1 orange = ₹ {tex}\frac{56}{20}{/tex} = 2.8
Selling price of 12 oranges = ₹ 35
Selling price of 1 orange = ₹ {tex}\frac{35}{12}{/tex} = 2.916
Gain = 2.916 - 2.8 = 0.1166
Gain% = {tex}\frac{(0.116)}{2.8} \times 100{/tex} = 4.1666 = {tex}4\frac{1}{6}\% {/tex}

questions.question: The cost of a flower vase is ₹120. If the shopkeeper sells it at a loss of 10%, find the price at which it is sold.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of flower vase = 120 ₹.
Sells at 10% loss = {tex}120 \times \frac{10}{100}{/tex} = 12
Then, selling price = Cost price - loss = 120 - 12 = 108

questions.question: Selling price of a toy car is ₹540. If the profit made by shopkeeper is 20%, what is the cost price of this toy?
questions.explanation:
Selling price of a toy car = ₹540
Cost Price = (S.P. {tex}\times{/tex} 100) {tex}\div{/tex} (100 + Profit %)
Then, Cost price = (540 {tex}\times{/tex} 100) {tex}\div{/tex}120 = 5400 {tex}\div{/tex} 12 = ₹450

questions.question: The marked price of a ceiling fan is ₹1250 and the shopkeeper allows a discount of 6% on it. Find the selling price of the fan.
questions.explanation:
Marked price of a ceiling fan = ₹1250
Discount of 6{tex}\% {/tex} = {tex}1250 \times \frac{6}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{7500}{100}{/tex} = 75 ₹.
Selling price of fan = 1250 - 75 = 1175 ₹.

questions.question: A trader marks his goods at 40% above the cost price and allows a discount of 25%. What is his gain?
questions.explanation:
Let C.P = ₹100
Marked price = ₹140
Discount = 25% of marked price 
= {tex}\frac{25}{100} \times 140{/tex} = ₹35
S.P = Marked price -Discount
= 140 - 35 = ₹105
Gain% = 105 - 100 = 5%

questions.question: A dealer purchased a washing machine for ₹7660. He allows a discount of 12% on its marked price and still gains 10%. Find the marked price of the machine.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of washing machine = ₹7660
Gains = 10%
Selling price = {tex}7660 \times [\frac{(100 + 10)}{100}]{/tex}
= {tex}7660 \times (\frac{11}{10}){/tex} = 8426 ₹
Let marked price = ₹ X
Selling price = Marked price {tex}\times{/tex} [{tex}\frac{(100 - discount)}{100}{/tex}]
8426 = Marked price {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac{88}{100}{/tex})
Marked price = {tex}\frac{842600}{88}{/tex} = ₹ 9575

questions.question: On selling a fan for ₹810. Sunil gains 8%. For how much did he purchase it?
questions.explanation:
Selling price of a fan = ₹810
Let C.P = 100 then S.P = 100 + 8 = 108
Cost price = {tex}810 \times (\frac{100}{108}){/tex}= 750

questions.question: The cost of one packet of balls having 20 balls is ₹100, what will be the cost of such 24 balls.
questions.explanation:
Cost of 20 balls = ₹100
Cost of 1 ball = {tex}\frac{100}{20}{/tex} = ₹5
Cost of 24 such balls = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = ₹120

questions.question: The cost of 9 bowls is ₹72. Find the cost of 6 such bowls.
questions.explanation:
The cost of 9 bowls = ₹72
The cost of 1 bowl = ₹72 {tex}\div{/tex} 9 = ₹8
The cost of 6 bowls = ₹8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = ₹ 48

questions.question: Out of 80 students in a class 30 are girls. Find the percentage of girls in class.
questions.explanation:
Total students = 80
Girls are = 30 
Girls % = {tex}\frac{30}{80} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{300}{8}{/tex} = 37.5%

questions.question: What percent of the total distance of 100km is 22km?
questions.explanation:
Let X% of 100km = 22km
(X {tex}\times{/tex} 100) {tex}\div{/tex} 100 = 22
X = 22 %

questions.question: To compare two quantities, the units must be the ________.
questions.explanation: To compare two quantities, the units must be the Same. If two quantities cannot be expressed in terms of the same unit, there cannot be a ratio between them.

questions.question: {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} = ________%
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{3} = {\text{ }} - - - - \% {/tex} 
{tex}\frac{1}{3} {/tex} =  {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{3}{/tex} = {tex}33\frac{1}{3}\% {/tex}

  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Find the ratio of 1m to 1 km.
Find the ratio of 1 week of Feb. to Feb. month of a non leap year.
questions.explanation:
1 week of Feb.: Feb. month of a non-leap year (non-leap year = 28 days)
7 : 28
1 : 4

questions.question: The loss in a company has decreased from 20 lacs to 5 lacs in one year. Find the loss decrease percentage.
questions.explanation:
Loss = 20 lacs - 5 lacs = 15 lacs
Loss % = {tex}\frac{15}{20} \times 100{/tex} = 75%

questions.question: A basket is full of fruits mangoes, oranges and apples. If 60% are mangoes, 10% are oranges than what is the percentage of apples.
questions.explanation:
Let total percentage of fruits = 100
% of mangoes = 60
% of oranges = 10
Then, % of apples = 100 - (60 + 10) = 100 - 70 = 30%

questions.question: Find the ratio of 40 days to 40 hrs.
questions.explanation:
40 days : 40 hrs
We know that 1 day = 24 hrs
40 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 : 40
Then, 24 : 1

questions.question: There are 25 radios. 16 of them are damaged. Find the percentage of damaged radios.
questions.explanation:
Total radios = 25
Damaged radios = 16 
% of damaged radios = {tex}\frac{16}{25} \times 100{/tex} = 64%

questions.question: In a survey of 40 students, 25% of students liked to play football. What is the number of students who liked to play football?
questions.explanation:
Total students = 40
25% of students liked to play football = {tex}40 \times \frac{25}{100}{/tex} = 10 
Students who liked to play football = 10

questions.question: {tex}\frac{1}{6} {/tex} = _______%
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{1}{6} = {\text{ }} - - - - \% {/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{6} {/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = {tex}\frac{100}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{50}{3}{/tex} = {tex}16\frac{2}{3}\% {/tex}

questions.question: A shirt with marked price ₹1000 was sold to a customer for ₹900. The % of decrease is ________.
questions.explanation:
Marked price of shirt = ₹1000
Sold at = ₹900
% of decrease = {tex}\frac{\text{Marked price-selling price}}{\text{Marked price}} \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}(\frac{(1000 - 900)}{1000}) \times 100{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{100}{1000} \times 100{/tex}
= 10%

questions.question: Convert into fractions {tex}24\frac{1}{2}\% {/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}24\frac{1}{2}\% {/tex} = {tex}\frac{{49}}{{200}}{/tex}

questions.question: Convert into decimals {tex}24\frac{1}{2}\% {/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}24\frac{1}{2}\% {/tex}= {tex}\frac{49}{200}{/tex} = 0.245

questions.question: Convert into decimals 39.2%.
questions.explanation: 39.2{tex}\% {/tex} = {tex}\frac{39.2}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{392}{1000}{/tex} = 0.392

questions.question: A man buys 3 oranges for ₹4 and sells 4 oranges for ₹6. Find his gain or loss percent.
questions.explanation:
C.P of 3 oranges = ₹4
C.P of 1 orange = ₹{tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} = 1.333
S.P of 4 oranges = ₹6
S.P of 1 orange = ₹{tex}\frac{6}{4}{/tex} = 1.5
Gain = S.P- C.P = 1.5 - 1.333 = 0.167
Gain% = {tex}\frac{0.167}{1.333} \times 100{/tex} = 12.5%

questions.question: In a village, 30{tex}\% {/tex} people are women, 40% are men rest are children what is the percentage of children in the village.
questions.explanation:
Total percentage = 100
percentage of children = 100 - (% of women + % of men)
= 100 - (30 + 40)
= 100 - 70
= 30%

questions.question: The population of Rajasthan is 570 lakh and population of U.P is 1,560 lakh in the same area. Find the ratio of their population.
questions.explanation:
Population of Rajasthan:Population of U.P 
570 lakh : 1,560 lakh
57 : 156
19 : 52

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Find 3% of 1hr in seconds.
questions.explanation:
3% of 1hr in seconds.
We know that 1 hr = 3600 sec
3% of 3600sec = {tex}\frac{3}{100} \times 3600{/tex} = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 36 = 108 sec

questions.question: In a village, 30% people are women, 40% are men rest are Children. What is the % of children in the village?
questions.explanation:
Total percentage = 100
percentage of children = 100 - (% of women + % of men)
= 100 - (30 + 40)
= 100 - 70
= 30%

questions.question: The ratio of 3 km to 300 m is ________.
questions.explanation:
3 km to 300 m
We know that 1km = 1000m
3 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000 m : 300 m
3000m : 300m
10 : 1

questions.question: The number of illiterate persons in a country decreased from 150 lakhs to 100 lakhs in 10 yrs. The decrease percentage is ________.
questions.explanation:
Decreased number of illiterate persons in a country = 150 lakhs - 100 lakhs = 50 lakhs
Decreased % ={tex}\frac{50}{150} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5000}{150}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{3}{/tex} = 33.3%

questions.question: Calculate 12% of 1200.
questions.explanation: 12% of 1200 = {tex}\frac{12}{100} \times 1200{/tex} = {tex}\frac{14400}{100}{/tex} = 144

questions.question: Calculate 30% of 300.
questions.explanation: 30% of 300 = {tex}\frac{30}{100} \times 300{/tex} = {tex}\frac{9000}{100}{/tex} = 90

questions.question: Out of 40 students in a hostel, 8 are going to market, 20 are going to watch T.V, rest of the students are studying. What percentage of students are studying?
questions.explanation:
Total students = 40
Students whose are going to market = 8, 
Students whose are going to watch T.V= 20
Students are studying = 40 - (20 + 8) = 12

percentage of them = {tex}\frac{12}{40} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1200}{40}{/tex} = 30%

questions.question: Find the ratio of ₹500 to 50,000 paise.
questions.explanation:
₹500 to 50,000 paise
We know that 1 ₹ = 100 paise
Then, 500 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 paise : 50,000 paise
50,000 paise : 50,000 paise
1:1

questions.question: Neelu got{tex}\frac{320}{400}{/tex} in her report card. Hari scored {tex}\frac{280}{400}{/tex} in his report card. Who scored more percentage of marks?
questions.explanation:
Neelu got {tex}\frac{320}{400}{/tex} in her report card.
Then, the percentage of Neelu = {tex}\frac{320}{400} \times 100{/tex} = 80%
Hari scored {tex}\frac{280}{400}{/tex} in his report card.
Then, the percentage of Hari = {tex}\frac{280}{400} \times 100{/tex} = 70%
Neelu scored more percentage of marks.

questions.question: Find the whole quantity if 5% of it is 600.
questions.explanation:
Let the quantity = X
5% of X = 600
{tex}X \times \frac{5}{100}{/tex} = 600
5X = 60000
X = 12000

questions.question: The ratio of 5 m to 50 cm is ________.
questions.explanation:
5 m to 50 cm 
We know that 1 m = 100 cm
5 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 cm : 50 cm
500 cm : 50 cm
10:1

questions.question: 0.6% expressed as fraction is ________.
questions.explanation: 0.6% = {tex}\frac{0.6}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{1000}{/tex}

questions.question: The cost of a flower vase is ₹120. If the shopkeeper sells it at a loss of 10%, the selling price is ________.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of a flower vase = ₹120
Sells at a loss of 10% = {tex}120 \times \frac{10}{100}{/tex} = 12
Selling price = Cost price - Loss = 120 - 12 = 108

questions.question: The Difference between the principal and amount is said to be the:
questions.explanation:
The Difference between the principal and amount is said to be the Interest.
We know that Interest = Amount - Principal

questions.question: Find out the numbers if 10% of number is 600.
questions.explanation:
Let a number = X
10% of number = 600
{tex}X \times \frac{10}{100}{/tex} = 600
{tex}\frac{X}{10}{/tex} = 600
X = 6000

questions.question: Find out the numbers if 12% of number is 1080.
questions.explanation:
Let a number = X
12% of number = 1080
{tex}X \times \frac{12}{100}{/tex} = 1080
12X = 108000
X = {tex}\frac{108000}{12}{/tex} = 9000

questions.question: A person divides his income in three equal parts if he gives 2 parts to Ram and 1 part to Shyam. What percentage of money he gives to Ram separately.
questions.explanation:
Total parts = 3
Ram's part = {tex}\frac{2}{3} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{200}{3}{/tex} = {tex}66\frac{2}{3}\% {/tex} 
Shyam's part = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{3}{/tex} = {tex}33\frac{1}{3}\% {/tex}

questions.question: On selling a table for ₹987. Ramesh loses 6%. For how much did he purchase it?
questions.explanation:
Let cost price = ₹100
Loss of 6% then selling price = ₹94
Selling price = ₹987
Then, the cost price = {tex}\frac{987 \times 100}{94}{/tex} = ₹1050

questions.question: The weight of one iron ball is 16 kg. Find the weight of 8 such iron balls.
questions.explanation:
Weight of one iron ball = 16 kg
Weight of 8 iron ball = 16 kg {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 128 kg

questions.question: Write {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}in the form of percentage.
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100 = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = 75%

questions.question: Convert 45% into fraction.
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{45}{100}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{9}{20}{/tex} ={tex}\frac{9}{{20}}{/tex}

questions.question: The cost of one packet of pencil having 46 pencils is ₹184 what will be the cost of such 98 pencils:
questions.explanation:
The cost of 46 pencils = ₹184
The cost of 1 pencil = ₹{tex}\frac{184}{46}{/tex} = ₹4
The cost of 98 pencils = ₹4 {tex}\times{/tex} 98 = ₹392

questions.question: The ratio of ₹3 to 30 paise is ________.
questions.explanation:
₹3 : 30 paise
We know that 1₹ = 100 paise
Then, 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 100 paise : 30 paise
= 300 : 30
= 10 :1

questions.question: The two ratios {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{8}{/tex} are ________.
questions.explanation:
For checking equality, we can do cross multiply.
{tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex} : {tex}\frac{3}{8}{/tex}
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 : 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
= 16 : 15
These are not equal.

questions.question: S.P of a toy is ₹540. If the profit made by the shopkeeper is 20%, the C.P of the toy is ________.
questions.explanation:
S.P of a toy = ₹540
Let C.P = ₹100
After 20% profit, S.P = ₹ 100 + 20 = ₹120
Then, C.P is = {tex}\frac{540 \times 100}{120}{/tex} = ₹ 450

questions.question: The sum of the Simple interest and the principal gives the _______.
questions.explanation:
The sum of the Simple interest and the principal gives the Amount.
We know that, Amount = Simple Interest + Principal

questions.question: Total numbers of beads in a bag are 20, if red beads are 8 and blue beads are 12, find out the percentage of blue colour of beads.
questions.explanation:
Total numbers of beads in a bag = 20
% of red beads = {tex}\frac{8}{20} \times 100{/tex} = 40 %
% of blue beads = {tex}\frac{12}{20} \times 100{/tex} = 60%

questions.question: A shopkeeper sells an article of ₹400, while he purchases it for ₹402. Find out loss percent of shopkeeper.
questions.explanation:
S.P of an article = ₹400
C.P of an article = ₹402
Loss = C.P - S.P = 402 - 400 = 2
Loss % = {tex}\frac{2}{402} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{201}{/tex} = 0.49%

questions.question: Rashmi buys a calculator for ₹720 and sells it at a loss of {tex}6\frac{2}{3}\% {/tex}. For how much does she sell it?
questions.explanation:
C. P of a calculator = ₹720
loss of {tex}6\frac{2}{3}\% {/tex} = {tex}720 \times \frac{20}{300}{/tex} = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 48
S.P of a calculator = C.P - Loss = ₹720 - ₹ 48 = ₹672

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The boys and girls in a school are in the ratio of 8 : 5. If the number of girls is 160, what is the total strength of the school?
questions.explanation:
Let total strength of school = X
No. of girls = {tex}(\frac{5}{8} + 5) \times X{/tex} = 160
{tex}\frac{5X}{13}{/tex} = 160
= 5X = 160 {tex}\times{/tex} 13
= X = 416
Thus, the total strength X = 416

questions.question: The simple interest on ₹5000 for 219 days at 4% per annum is
questions.explanation:
Total days in one year = 365 days
Simple Interest = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{5000 \times 219 \times 4}{365 \times 100}{/tex}
= 120

questions.question: Find the simple interest on ₹2500 for 2 years 6 months at 6% per annum.
questions.explanation:
Time = 2 years 6 month = 2 years {tex}\frac{6}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{5}{2}{/tex}
Simple Interest = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2500 \times 5 \times 6}{2 \times 100}{/tex}
= ₹375

questions.question: What sum will amount to ₹4590 at 12% per annum simple interest in 3 years?
questions.explanation:
Let the sum = ₹ P
Amount = Simple Interest + Principal
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{p \times 12 \times 3}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{36P}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4590 = {tex}\frac{136P}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{4590 \times 100}{136}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = ₹3375

questions.question: In what time will ₹1860 amount to ₹2278.50, if simple interest is calculated at 9% per annum?
questions.explanation:
Sum = ₹1860
Amount = ₹2278.50
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 = {tex}\frac{1860 \times 9 \times T}{100}{/tex} + 1860
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 = {tex}\frac{1674T}{10}{/tex} + 1860
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2278.50 - 1860 = 167.4T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 418.50 = 167.4T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T = {tex}\frac{418.5}{167.4}{/tex} = 2.5 = Means 2 years 6 month

questions.question: At what rate percent per annum will ₹1650 amount to ₹2046 in 3 years?
questions.explanation:
Sum = ₹1650
Amount = ₹2046
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 = {tex}\frac{1650 \times 3 \times R}{100}{/tex} + 1650
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 = {tex}\frac{495R}{10}{/tex} + 1650
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2046 - 1650 = {tex}\frac{495R}{10}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 396 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 495R
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = {tex}\frac{3960}{495}{/tex} = 8%

questions.question: At what rate percent per annum simple interest will a sum treble itself in 16 years?
questions.explanation:
Let sum = P
Simple interest = 2P
S.I = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2P = {tex}\frac{P \times R \times 16}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 200P = 16PR
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{200}{16}{/tex} = R
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} R = 12.5%

questions.question: In what time will ₹1600 amount to ₹1768 at 6% per annum simple interest?
questions.explanation:
Sum = ₹1600
Amount = ₹1768
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768 = {tex}\frac{1600 \times 6 \times T}{100}{/tex} + 1600
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768 = 96T + 1600
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 1768-1600 = 96T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 168 = 96T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} T = {tex}\frac{168}{96}{/tex} = 1.75 = Means 1 years 9 month

questions.question: A sum amounts to ₹3720 in 8 months at 5% per annum simple interest. The sum is
questions.explanation:
Let Sum = P
Time = 8 month = {tex}\frac{8}{12}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
Amount = ₹3720
Amount = S.I + Sum
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{P \times 5 \times 2}{3 \times 100}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{P}{30}{/tex} + P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3720 = {tex}\frac{31P}{30}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} P = {tex}\frac{3720 \times 30}{31}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{111600}{31}{/tex} = ₹3600

questions.question: At what rate percent per annum simple interest will a sum be double itself in 8 years?
questions.explanation:
Let Principal = P
Then, S.I = P
Time = 8 years
Rate = ?
S.I = {tex}\frac{PRT}{100}{/tex}
P = {tex}\frac{PR \times 8}{100}{/tex}
R = {tex}\frac{100P}{8P}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{8}{/tex} = 12.5%

questions.question: Mala has a collection of bangles. She has 20 gold bangles and 10 silver bangles. What is the percentage of bangles of each type?
questions.explanation:
Total bangles = 30
Percentage of gold bangles = {tex}\frac{20}{30} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{200}{3}{/tex} = 66.666 = 66%
Percentage of silver bangles = {tex}\frac{10}{30} \times 100{/tex} = {tex}\frac{100}{3}{/tex} = 33.333 = 34%

questions.question: Rohan bought a second hand refrigerator for ₹2,500, then spent ₹500 on its repairs and sold it for ₹3,300. Find his loss or gain percent.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of a second hand refrigerator = ₹2,500
Spent Rs 500 on its repairs then the cost price = ₹2500 + 500 = ₹3000
Selling price of it = ₹3300
Gain = S.P - C.P = 3300 - 3000 = 300
Gain % = {tex}\frac{300}{3000} \times 100{/tex} = 10%

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 200 bulbs for ₹10 each. However, 5 bulbs were fused and had to be thrown away. The remaining were sold at ₹12 each. Find the gain or loss %.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of 1 bulb = ₹10
Then, cost price of 200 bulbs = ₹200 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = ₹2000
5 bulbs were fused then, remaining bulbs = 200 - 5 = 195
Remaining were sold at ₹12 each then, Selling price of remaining bulbs = 195 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = ₹2340
Gain = ₹2340 - 2000 = 340
Gain % = {tex}\frac{340}{2000} \times 100 {/tex} = 17%

questions.question: During a sale, a shop offered a discount of 10% on the marked prices of all the items. What would a customer have to pay for a pair of jeans marked at ₹1450 and two shirts marked at ₹850 each?
questions.explanation:
Cost of two shirts = ₹ 850 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = ₹1700
Customer have to pay for a pair of jeans and two shirts = 1450 - [1450 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac{10}{100}{/tex}]
= 1450 - 145 = ₹1305
Customer have to pay for two shirts = 1700 - {2 {tex}\times{/tex} [850 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\frac{10}{100}{/tex})]}
=1700 - {2 {tex}\times{/tex} 85}
=1700 - 170 = ₹1530
Total ₹ = 1305 + 1530 = ₹ 2,835

questions.question: Find the ratio of 5 m to 10 km.
questions.explanation:
5 m : 10 km
We know that 1 km = 1000m
5m : 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000m
5m : 10000m
1 : 2000

questions.question: An item marked at ₹840 is sold for ₹714. What is the discount %?
questions.explanation:
Marked price of an item = ₹840
Selling price = ₹714
Discount = ₹840 - 714 = ₹126
Discount% = {tex}\frac{126}{840} \times 100{/tex} = 15%

questions.question: Find the ratio of speed of a cycle 20 km per hour to the speed of scooter 30 km per hour.
questions.explanation:
Speed of a cycle : speed of scooter.
20 km per hour : 30 km per hour
= 20 : 30
= 2 : 3

questions.question: Find the ratio of speed of a car 50 km per hour to the speed of scooter 40 km per hour.
questions.explanation:
speed of a car : speed of scooter 
50 km per hour : 40 km per hour
= 50 : 40
= 5:4

questions.question: The price of a car was ₹3,40,000 last year. It has increased by 20% this year. What is the price now?
questions.explanation:
Last year, Cost price of a car = ₹3,40,000 
Increased by 20% this year = {tex}340000 \times \frac{20}{100}{/tex} = ₹68000
Now the price is = ₹340000 + 68000 = ₹4,08,000

questions.question: The price of a car was ₹5,00,000 last year. It has increased by 10% this year. What is the price now?
questions.explanation:
Last year, Cost price of a car  = ₹5,00,000
Increased by 10% this year = ₹ {tex}500000 \times \frac{10}{100}{/tex} = ₹50000
Now, the price is = ₹ 500000 + 50000 = ₹5,50,000

questions.question: A picnic is being planned in a school for Class VI. Girls are 40% of the total number of students and are 20 in number. Find the ratio of the number of girls to the number of boys in the class.
questions.explanation:
Let total % = 100
Girls are 40% then boys will be 60%
Ratio of Girls and boys = 40 : 60
= 2:3

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 300 bulbs for ₹10 each. However, 10 bulbs were fused and had to be thrown away. The remaining were sold at ₹12 each. Find the gain or loss %.
questions.explanation:
If the cost of 1 bulb = ₹10
Then, Cost of 300 bulbs = 300 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 3000 ₹
10 bulbs were fused then remaining = 300 - 10 = 290
Remaining were sold at ₹12 each then, selling price of remaining = 290 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = ₹3480
Profit = S.P - C.P = 3480 - 3000 = 480 ₹
Profit % = {tex}\frac{480}{3000} \times 100{/tex} = 16%

questions.question: A shopkeeper purchased 500 pieces for ₹20 each. However 50 pieces were spoiled in the way and had to be thrown away. The remaining were sold at ₹25 each. Find the gain or loss %.
questions.explanation:
If the cost of 1 piece = ₹ 20
Then, Cost of 500 pieces = 500 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 10000 ₹
50 pieces were spoiled in the way then remaining = 500 - 50 = 450
Remaining were sold at ₹25 each then, selling price of remaining = 450 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = ₹ 11250
Profit = S.P - C.P = 11250 - 10000 = 1250 ₹
Profit % = {tex}\frac{1250}{10000} \times 100{/tex} = 12.5%

questions.question: The population of a city was 40,000 in the year 1999. It increased at a rate of 10% p.a. Find the population at the end of the year 2000.
questions.explanation:
10% of 40000 population = {tex}\frac{10}{100} \times 40000{/tex} = 4000
Population in 2000 = 40000 + 4000 = 44000

questions.question: 72% of 25 students are good in mathematics. How many are not good in mathematics?
questions.explanation:
Given 72% of students are good in English.
So, 100 - 72 = 28% of students are not good in English.

questions.question: 15% of 200 students are good in English. How many are not good in English?
questions.explanation:
Given 15% of students are good in English.
So, 100 - 15 = 85% of students are not good in English.

questions.question: Sheela bought two fans for ₹1200 each. She sold one at a loss of 5% and the other at a profit of 10%. Find the selling price of each. Also find out the total profit or loss.
questions.explanation:
Cost price of one fan= ₹1200
Let C.P of fan = ₹100
Loss of 5% on one fan then S.P. is ₹95
Then, S.P. of one fan = {tex}\frac{1200 \times 95}{100}{/tex} = ₹ 1140
Profit of 10% on second fan then S.P. is ₹110
Then, S.P. of second fan = ({tex}\frac{1200 \times 110}{100}{/tex}) = ₹1320
Total S.P = ₹. (1140 + 1320) = 2460 ₹
Total C.P = ₹ (1200 + 1200) = ₹ 2400
Profit = S.P - C.P = 2460 - 2400 = ₹ 60

questions.question: A milkman sold two of his buffaloes for ₹20,000 each. On one he made a gain of 5% and on the other a loss of 10%. Find his overall gain or loss.
questions.explanation:
Selling price of each buffalo = ₹20000
Then, C.P. of one buffalo with 5% profit = {tex}(20000\times100) \over 105{/tex} = ₹19047.62 {tex}[C.P={S.P.\times100 \over (100+profit \%)}]{/tex} 
Then, C.P. of second buffalo with 10% loss = {tex}(20000\times100) \over 90{/tex} = ₹ 22222.22 {tex}[C.P.={S.P.\times100 \over (100-loss \%)}]{/tex} 
Total C.P = ₹ (19047.62 + 22222.22) = 41269.84 ₹
Total S.P = ₹ (20000 + 20000) = ₹40000
Thus, overall Loss = C.P - S.P = 41269.84 - 40000 = ₹1269.84

questions.question: Find the ratio of 40 paise to ₹8.
questions.explanation:
1 rupee = 100 paise
8 = 800 paise
Ratio: {tex}\frac{40}{800}{/tex} = 1:20

questions.question: Find the ratio of 60 paise to ₹9.
questions.explanation:
1 rupee = 100 paise
₹9 = 900 paise
Ratio: {tex}\frac{60}{900}{/tex} = 1 : 15

questions.question: Associative property is not followed in ________.
questions.explanation:
The associative property states that you can add or multiply regardless of how numbers are grouped.
By grouping we mean, how you use parenthesis.
This property doesn't hold true for rational numbers. As they are in the form of {tex}{p \over q}{/tex}

questions.question: ________ is the identity for the addition of rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Any rational number added to 0 yields the same number.

questions.question: ________ is the multiplicative identity for rational numbers.
questions.explanation: Any rational number multiplied by 1 will yield the same number.

questions.question: The additive inverse of {tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse of a number is what you add to it so that sum of both becomes zero.
Additive inverse of a number is opposite in sign to it.
So, additive inverse of {tex}{7 \over 5}{/tex} is -{tex}{7 \over 5}{/tex} 
{tex}{7 \over 5}{/tex} + (-{tex}{7 \over 5}{/tex}) = 0

questions.question: Zero has ________ reciprocal.
questions.explanation: Any number divided by 0 is undefined.

questions.question: The numbers ________ and ________ are their own reciprocals.
questions.explanation: The reciprocal of 1 is {tex}\frac{1}{1} = 1{/tex} and the reciprocal of -1 is {tex}\frac{-1}{1} = -1{/tex}

questions.question: The reciprocal of – 5 is ________.
questions.explanation:
When the product of two numbers is one, they are called reciprocals of each other.
So, reciprocal of -5 is -{tex}{1 \over 5}{/tex}.

questions.question: Reciprocal of {tex}\frac{1}{x}{/tex}, where x {tex} \ne {/tex} 0 is ________.
questions.explanation:
When product of two numbers is one, they are called reciprocal of each other.
Reciprocal of {tex}{1 \over x}{/tex} = x.

questions.question: Which among the following is a rational number equivalent to {tex}\frac { 5 } { 3 }{/tex}?
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac { 25 } { 15 }{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{5} \times \frac{3}{7} \times \frac{{15}}{{16}} \times \left( {\frac{{ - 14}}{9}} \right){/tex}
questions.explanation:
= {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{5} \times \frac{3}{7} \times \frac{{15}}{{16}} \times \left( {\frac{{ - 14}}{9}} \right){/tex} 
= {tex}{-4\over 5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{3\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{5\over 8}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} -{tex}{7\over 3}{/tex} 
= {tex}{4\over 5}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{5\over 8}{/tex} 
= {tex}{4\over 8}{/tex} 
= {tex}{1\over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: The sum of the rational numbers {tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{{16}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} is
questions.explanation: {tex}\frac{-5}{16} + \frac{7}{12} = \frac {-15+28}{48} = \frac{13}{48}{/tex}

questions.question: What number should be added to {tex}\frac{7}{{12}}{/tex} to get {tex}\frac{4}{15}{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{7}{12} + x = \frac{4}{15} {/tex} 
x = {tex}\frac{4}{15}-\frac{7}{12}{/tex} 
x = {tex} \frac{16-35}{60} = \frac{-19}{60}{/tex}

questions.question: Rewrite {tex}\frac{{ - 44}}{{72}}{/tex} in the simplest form.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{-44}{72} = \frac{-22}{36} {/tex} (Divide numerator and denominator by 2)
{tex}\frac{-22}{36} = \frac{-11}{18}{/tex} (Divide numerator and denominator by 2 again)

questions.question: Sum of two rational numbers is –8, one number is {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}, find other.
questions.explanation:
Let the other number be x
Now, given one number is {tex}{3\over 4}{/tex} 
So, x + {tex}{3\over 4}{/tex}= -8
x = -8 - {tex}{3\over 4}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{-32-3 \over 4}{/tex} 
x = -{tex}{35\over 4}{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the rational number is positive? {tex}\frac{3}{7},-\frac{3}{7},-\frac{4}{7}, \frac{-5}{7}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since, all other rational numbers are negative.
So, {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} is positive rational number.

questions.question: Write the rational number whose numerator is 4 {tex} \times {/tex} (-7) and denominator is (3 – 7) {tex} \times {/tex} (15 - 11).
questions.explanation:
Given, 
The numerator is 4 {tex} \times {/tex} (-7)
The denominator is (3 - 7) {tex} \times {/tex} (15 - 11)
So the rational number = {tex}{numerator \over denominator}{/tex} 
= {tex}{4 × ( – 7) \over (3-7) × (15-11) }{/tex} 
= {tex}{-28 \over (-4) × (4) }{/tex} [- sign in numerator denominator canceled out]
= {tex}{28 \over 16 }{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3}{/tex} ________{tex}\frac{-1}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex} = 0.75 
{tex}{1 \over 4}{/tex} = 0.25
But since, sign is negative with both numbers so, 
If Numbers are given with negative sign, so, the numbers closer to zero would be larger.
-{tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex} <  -{tex}{1 \over 4}{/tex}

questions.question: 0 ________ {tex}\frac{-7}{6}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
0 is always greater than any negative number.
So 0 > {tex}{-7 \over 6}{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}\frac{5}{{ - 11}}{/tex} ________ {tex}\frac{-5}{11}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Placing negative signs with denominators or numerators does not make any difference.
So, both the given numbers are equal.
{tex}{-5 \over 11}{/tex} = {tex}{-5 \over 11}{/tex}

questions.question: Write the following rational numbers in ascending order: {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{7},\frac{{ - 3}}{2},\frac{{ - 3}}{4}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since, the numerator is equal for all three numbers.
So, greater the denominator, smaller the number.
But, since here, signs are negative, 
So, with negative sign, larger the denominator, larger the number.
{tex}{-3 \over 2}{/tex} < {tex}{-3 \over 4}{/tex}< {tex}{-3 \over 7}{/tex}

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{{13}}{4} + \frac{{ - 12}}{5} + \frac{{ - 3}}{4} + \frac{2}{3} + \frac{{ - 3}}{5} + \frac{4}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{13}}{4} + \frac{{ - 12}}{5} + \frac{{ - 3}}{4} + \frac{2}{3} + \frac{{ - 3}}{5} + \frac{4}{3}{/tex} 
Let us bring numbers with same denominator together,
= ({tex}{13\over 4}{/tex}+ {tex}{-3\over 4}{/tex} ) + ({tex}{-12\over 5}{/tex} + {tex}{-3\over 5}{/tex}) + ({tex}{2\over 3}{/tex} + {tex}{4\over 3}{/tex})
= {tex}{10\over 4}{/tex} + {tex}{-15\over 5}{/tex} + {tex}{6\over 3}{/tex} 
= {tex}{5\over 2}{/tex} - 3 + 2
= {tex}{5\over 2}{/tex} -1
= {tex}{5 -2 \over 2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{3\over 2}{/tex}

questions.question: From his home, Rahul walks {tex}\frac{6}{7}{/tex} km towards school and then returns {tex}\frac{5}{6}{/tex} km on the same way towards his home to reach a landmark. Where will he be now from his home?
questions.explanation:
Rahul walks {tex}{6\over 7}{/tex}km towards school in forward direction.
Rahuul walks {tex}{5\over 6}{/tex}km in backward direction to a landmark.
So, distance of his home from the landmark would be
= {tex}{6\over 7}{/tex} - {tex}{5\over 6}{/tex} 
= {tex}{36-35\over 42}{/tex}  {taking LCM of 7, 6 which is 42}
= {tex}{1\over 42}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the reciprocal of {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3} \times \frac{{ - 15}}{6}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Solving, {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{3} \times \frac{{ - 15}}{6}{/tex} 
= {tex}{15 \over 3 ×6}{/tex} 
= {tex}{5 \over 6}{/tex} 
So, reciprocal of a number is the number which when multiplied with given number gives product as 1.
So, reciprocal of {tex}{5 \over 6}{/tex} is {tex}{6 \over 5}{/tex} 
{tex}{5 \over 6}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{6 \over 5}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: Product of two rational numbers is {tex}\frac{{ - 8}}{9}{/tex}, one is {tex}\frac{{ - 10}}{3}{/tex}, find other.
questions.explanation:
Let other rational number be p.
Given, p {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{ - 10}}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{ - 8}}{9}{/tex} 
p = {tex}\frac{{ - 8×3}}{-9×10}{/tex} 
p = {tex}\frac{{ 4}}{15}{/tex}

questions.question: Reduce {tex}\frac{{ - 63}}{{99}}{/tex} to the standard form.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{ - 63}}{{99}}{/tex} 
Reducing it to standard form,
Here, we can divide numerator and denominator by 9.
= {tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{{11}}{/tex}

questions.question: Write the rational number whose denominator is the smallest 2 digit number and the numerator is the greatest 3 digit number.
questions.explanation:
The denominator is the smallest 2 digit number, which is 10.
The numerator is the greatest 3 digit number, which is 999.
Thus rational number is, {tex}{numerator \over denominator}{/tex} = {tex}{999 \over 10}{/tex}

questions.question: The rational number {tex}\frac{9}{1}{/tex} in integer is ________.
questions.explanation:
Rational number = {tex}\frac{9}{1}{/tex}
Since 1 in the denominator does not make any difference to the number.
So, {tex}\frac{9}{1}{/tex} can also be written as 9.
Here, 9 is an integer.

questions.question: Numerator of {tex}5\frac{3}{4}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}5\frac{3}{4}{/tex} can be written as 5 + {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} 
So here we multiply 5 and 4 and add 3 to the product.
So {tex}5\frac{3}{4}{/tex} can also be written as {tex}\frac{23}{4}{/tex} 
Thus, the numerator is 23.

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{5}{/tex} as a rational number with denominator 15 is _______.
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{5}{/tex} 
If we want o make denominator 15, then we need to multiply numerator and denominator with 3
So, {tex}\frac{{ - 3 ×3}}{5× 3}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{{ - 9}}{15}{/tex}

questions.question: Find out two rational numbers between {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{4}{/tex} and 0.
questions.explanation:
Rational numbers between {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{4}{/tex} and 0.
So we can say, {tex}\frac{{ - 2}}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{4}{/tex} lie between {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{4}{/tex} and 0.

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\left[ {\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{{ - 5}}{4}} \right] + \left[ {\frac{{ - 10}}{3} \times \frac{5}{2}} \right] - \left[ {\frac{{ - 16}}{3} \times \frac{{ - 55}}{{32}}} \right]{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
={tex}\left[ {\frac{2}{3} \times \frac{{ - 5}}{4}} \right] + \left[ {\frac{{ - 10}}{3} \times \frac{5}{2}} \right] - \left[ {\frac{{ - 16}}{3} \times \frac{{ - 55}}{{32}}} \right]{/tex} 
= {tex}{-5 \over 6}{/tex} + {tex}{-50 \over 6}{/tex} - {tex}{55 \over 6}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-5-50-55 \over 6}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-110 \over 6}{/tex} 
= {tex} - 18\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: Mayank reads {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} of a storybook on the first day and {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} of the book on the second day. What part of the storybook is yet to be read by Mayank?
questions.explanation:
Given,
Mayank reads {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} of a storybook on the first day and {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} of the book on the second day.
Now, the whole book would be considered as 1 part.
So, part of the book left to read = 1- ({tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex})
= 1- ({tex}\frac{3+4}{12}{/tex})
= 1 - {tex}\frac{7}{12}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{12-7}{12}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{5}{12}{/tex}

questions.question: The reciprocal of a positive rational number is ________.
questions.explanation:
Reciprocal of positive rational number will always be positive.
Reciprocal of any number is the number which when multiplied with the given number gives product as 1.

questions.question: What number should be subtracted from{tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{5}{/tex} to get -2?
questions.explanation:
Let the number to be subtracted be x.
Now {tex}\frac{-3}{5}{/tex} - x = -2
-x  = -2 + {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-10}{5}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}( taking 5 as LCM)
= {tex}\frac{-10+3}{5}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-7}{5}{/tex} 
So, x = {tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: Which of the rational numbers {tex}\frac{{ - 11}}{{28}},\frac{{ - 5}}{7},\frac{9}{{ - 14}},\frac{{29}}{{ - 42}}{/tex} is the greatest?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{ - 11}}{{28}},\frac{{ - 5}}{7},\frac{9}{{ - 14}},\frac{{29}}{{ - 42}}{/tex} 
{tex}{-11\over 28}{/tex}= -0.39
{tex}{-5\over 7}{/tex} = -0.71
{tex}{-9\over 14}{/tex} = -0.64
{tex}{-29\over 42}{/tex} = -0.69
So, we can say {tex}{-11\over 28}{/tex} is greatest.

questions.question: Which of the rational numbers {tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{{16}},\frac{{ - 13}}{{24}},\frac{3}{{ - 4}},\frac{7}{{ - 12}}{/tex} is the smallest?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{{16}},\frac{{ - 13}}{{24}},\frac{3}{{ - 4}},\frac{7}{{ - 12}}{/tex} 
{tex}{-5\over 16}{/tex} = -0.31
{tex}{-13\over 24}{/tex} = -0.54
{tex}{-3\over 4}{/tex} = -0.75
{tex}{-7\over 12}{/tex} = -0.58
Thus, since sign is negative so, -0.75 will be least.

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{2}{3} + \frac{{ - 4}}{5} + \frac{7}{{15}} + \frac{{ - 11}}{{20}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{2}{3} + \frac{{ - 4}}{5} + \frac{7}{{15}} + \frac{{ - 11}}{{20}}{/tex} 
Taking LCM of denominator which is 180
= {tex}{120\over 180}{/tex} + {tex}{-144\over 180}{/tex} + {tex}{84\over 180}{/tex} + {tex}{-99\over 180}{/tex} 
={tex}{120-144+84-99\over 180}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-39\over 180}{/tex} (since it has 3 as common factor in numerator and denominator, we will divide both sides by 3)
= {tex}{-13\over 60}{/tex}

questions.question: Rational number {tex}\frac{3}{{40}}{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Given, {tex}\frac{3}{{40}}{/tex} 
So, we need to divide 3 by 40.
Which gives {tex}\frac{3}{{40}}{/tex} = 0.075

questions.question: A rational number between 3 and 4 is:
questions.explanation:
Number between 3 and 4 is 3.5.
Now, it to be written in the form of rational number.
So 3.5 = {tex}\frac{7}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: A rational number between {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}is:
questions.explanation:
Number between {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{3.5}{{5}}{/tex} 
so, {tex}\frac{3.5}{{5}}{/tex} is written as {tex}\frac{7}{{10}}{/tex}

questions.question: A rational number between {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
To find a  number between two numbers, you can add both numbers and then divide by 2.
So, you get an exact middle number.
So, here adding {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} + {tex}{3 \over 4}{/tex} 
Taking LCM as 4.
= {tex}{2+3 \over 4}{/tex} 
= {tex}{5 \over 4}{/tex} 
Now, dividing it by 2.
So, {tex}{5 \over 8}{/tex} is the required rational number between {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} and {tex}{3 \over 4}{/tex}.

questions.question: The multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{7}{{40}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
The multiplicative inverse of a number is such number which when multiplies with the given numbers produces product as 1.
So, The multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{7}{{40}}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{{40}}{7}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{7}{{40}}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{40}}{7}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: Subtract {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{2}{/tex} from {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} - ({tex}\frac{-1}{2}{/tex})
{tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(two negative signs together becomes positive)
Taking LCM of 5 and 2 as 10
= {tex}\frac{4×2}{10}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1×5}{10}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{8+5}{10}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{13}{10}{/tex}

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{7}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{x}{-21}{/tex} are equivalent rational numbers.
questions.explanation:
Given,
{tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{7}=\frac{x}{{ - 21}}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{-3 × -21 \over 7}{/tex} 
= -3 {tex}\times{/tex} -3
= 9

questions.question:
Identify the smallest rational number.
{tex}\frac{5}{{ - 6}}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{{ - 9}}{{12}}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{{ - 8}}{9}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{5}{{ - 6}}{/tex} = -0.83
{tex}\frac{{ - 9}}{{12}}{/tex} = -0.75
{tex}\frac{{ - 8}}{9}{/tex} = -0.77
{tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} since it is positive so this cannot be the smallest from above three negative numbers, we can see  {tex}\frac{5}{{ - 6}}{/tex} is the smallest.

tests.name: Rational Numbers Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 7 Rational Numbers CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The integer -8 as rational number is ________.
questions.explanation:
1 in the denominator does not make any difference to the number.
So, if we want to write -8 in the form of a rational number.
We can write it as {tex}\frac{{ - 8}}{1}{/tex}.

questions.question: The product of a rational number with its reciprocal is always ________.
questions.explanation: As per multiplicative inverse, the product of a number and its reciprocal is 1.

questions.question: Simplify: {tex}\frac{{10}}{{13}} \times \frac{{26}}{{15}} + \frac{{13}}{{25}} \times \frac{{10}}{{13}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
= {tex}\frac{{10}}{{13}} \times \frac{{26}}{{15}} + \frac{{13}}{{25}} \times \frac{{10}}{{13}}{/tex} 
= {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex} + {tex}{2 \over 5}{/tex} {cancelling 26 and 13 by 13 and 10, 15, 25 can be reduced with 5}
= {tex}{20 + 6 \over 15}{/tex} {taking LCM of 3, 5}
= {tex}{26 \over 15}{/tex} 
= {tex}1\frac{{11}}{{15}}{/tex}

questions.question: Seema spends {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} of her pocket money. She spends {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of it on a book, {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}on a movie and the remaining amount on a dress. What part of her pocket money did she spend on the dress?
questions.explanation:
She spends on a book = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
She spends on a movie = {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} 
Part of her pocket money did she spend on the dress= x(assume)
Now, according to question= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex} + x = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} 
x = {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}- {tex}\frac{1}{6}{/tex}- {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} 
x = {tex}\frac{9-2-6}{12}{/tex} { Taking LCM of 4, 6, 2 as 12}
x = {tex}\frac{1}{12}{/tex}

questions.question: The multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{{11}}{{35}}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Multiplicative inverse of a number is the number which when multiplied with the given number produces a product as 1.
So, Multiplicative inverse of {tex}\frac{{11}}{{35}}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{{35}}{{11}}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{11}}{{35}}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{{35}}{{11}}{/tex} = 1

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{5} = \frac{8}{x}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given, 
{tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{5} = \frac{8}{x}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{-8×5 \over 1}{/tex} 
x = -40

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{7}{{ - 3}} = \frac{x}{6}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given,
{tex}\frac{7}{{ - 3}} = \frac{x}{6}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{7×6 \over -3}{/tex} 
= {tex}{7×2 \over -1}{/tex} 
= -14

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{3}{5} = \frac{x}{{ - 25}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given,
{tex}\frac{3}{5} = \frac{x}{{ - 25}}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{3×-25 \over 5}{/tex} 
= {tex}{3×-5 \over 1}{/tex} 
= -15

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{{13}}{6} = \frac{{ - 65}}{x}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Given,
{tex}\frac{{13}}{6} = \frac{{ - 65}}{x}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{-65×6 \over 13}{/tex} 
= -5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= -30

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{{16}}{x}{/tex} = -4.
questions.explanation:
Given,
{tex}\frac{{16}}{x} {/tex} = -4
x = {tex}{16\over -4}{/tex} 
x = -4

questions.question: Find x such that {tex}\frac{{ - 48}}{x}{/tex} = 2.
questions.explanation:
Given,
{tex}\frac{{ - 48}}{x}{/tex} = 2
x = {tex}{-48 \over 2}{/tex} 
x = -24

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\frac{9}{2} \times \frac{{ - 4}}{3}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{9}{2} \times \frac{{ - 4}}{3}{/tex} = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} -2 {reducing 4 with 2 and 9 with 3}
= -6

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\frac{3}{{ - 5}} \times \frac{{ - 5}}{{ - 3}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{3}{{ - 5}} \times \frac{{ - 5}}{{ - 3}}{/tex} 
Cancelling -5 with -5 and 3 with -3
So, ans is -1

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\frac{3}{{10}} \times \frac{{ - 2}}{3}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{3}{{10}} \times \frac{{ - 2}}{3}{/tex} 
Cancelling 3 with 3 and reducing 10 with -2
= {tex}{-2\over 10}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-1\over 5}{/tex}

questions.question: Find the value of {tex} - 6 \div \frac{2}{3}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
={tex} - 6 \div \frac{2}{3}{/tex} 
= -6 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{3 \over 2}{/tex} 
= -3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= -9

questions.question: Find the value of {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{8} \div \frac{3}{4}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
={tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{8} \div \frac{3}{4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{{ - 1}}{8} \ × \frac{4}{3}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-1 \over 2×3}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-1 \over 6}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by P
questions.explanation:
Since, between 0 and 1, there are 5 numbers with equal distance between them.
So, distance between any two number is {tex}{1\over 5}{/tex}
Now, P is 3 points away from1, so p would be {tex}\frac{3}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by Q
questions.explanation:
Since there are 5 numbers between 0 and 1
so, each of them has {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} distance between them.
Now, since Q is 4th from 0,
so Q = {tex}\frac{4}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by R
questions.explanation:
Since there are 5 numbers between 0 and -1
so, each number is at {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
Since R is next to 0 and left to 0,
R= {tex} - \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by S
questions.explanation:
Since there are 5 numbers equal distant from 0 and -1
so, each of them are at {tex} \frac{1}{5}{/tex}
since S is 3rd from 0,
so, S is {tex} - \frac{3}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by T
questions.explanation:
Since there are 5 numbers equal distant from 0 and 1.
so every number is a {tex} \frac{1}{5}{/tex} difference
since T is 2 numbers away from 0
so, it is {tex}\frac{2}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by U
questions.explanation:
There are 5 numbers from 0 and -1
so all are equidistant at {tex}-\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
Since, U is fourth from 0 so,
U = {tex}-\frac{4}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by V.
questions.explanation:
there are 5 numbers equal distant from 0 and -1.
so, each would be at distance of {tex} - \frac{1}{5}{/tex}
since, V is second from 0,
V = {tex} - \frac{2}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The points P, Q, R, S, T, U and V on the number line are such that, US = SV = VR, and WT = TP = PQ. The rational number represented by W
questions.explanation:
there are 5 numbers between 0 and 1 at equal distance.
so, each is at distance of {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
Now, W is next to 0 
W = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}

questions.question: The additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{7}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse is the number which when added to the given number gives the result as 0.
So, Additive inverse of -{tex}\frac{4}{7}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{4}{7}{/tex} 
-{tex}\frac{4}{7}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{4}{7}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: Write down the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse is the number, which when added to the given number gives result as 0.
So, additive inverse of {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} is {tex} - \frac{3}{7}{/tex} 
{tex} - \frac{3}{7}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{3}{7}{/tex} = 0

questions.question:
Identify the greatest rational number.
{tex}\frac{5}{7}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{{450}}{{ - 7}}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{{21}}{/tex}, -{tex}\frac{{29}}{{14}}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\frac{{450}}{{ - 7}}{/tex}, {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{{21}}{/tex} , -{tex}\frac{{29}}{{14}}{/tex} are negative numbers, so they cannot be greatest among given numbers.
So, {tex}\frac{5}{7}{/tex} is the greatest number.

questions.question: Find the product of {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{5} \times \frac{{35}}{7} \times \frac{{ - 1}}{6}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
= {tex}\frac{{ - 3}}{5} \times \frac{{35}}{7} \times \frac{{ - 1}}{6}{/tex} 
(reducing 6 with -3 and 35 with 5)
= {tex}{-1 × 7 × -1 \over 7 × 2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: {tex}\frac{7}{5}{/tex} + ..... = {tex}\frac{7}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Let the number to be filed is x.
So, {tex}{7 \over 5}{/tex} + x = {tex}{7 \over 3}{/tex} 
x = {tex}{7 \over 3}{/tex} - {tex}{7 \over 5}{/tex} 
= {tex}{35-21 \over 15}{/tex} { taking LCM of 3 and 5 as 15}
= {tex}\frac{{14}}{{15}}{/tex}

questions.question: If 35 shirts of equal size can be stitched from {tex}\frac{{49}}{2}{/tex} metres of cloth, what is the length of the cloth required for each shirt? Find the length of cloth required for 4 shirts of equal size.
questions.explanation:
Given,
35 shirts of equal size can be stitched from {tex}\frac{{49}}{2}{/tex}metres of cloth
So, the length of the cloth required for each shirt = {tex}\frac{{49}}{2×35}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{{7}}{2×5}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{{7}}{10}{/tex}m
Thus, cloth required for 4 shirts = {tex}\frac{{7}}{10}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 4  = {tex}\frac{{7×2}}{5}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{{14}}{5}{/tex}m = 2.8 m

questions.question: Write down the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{9}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse of a number is the number which when added with the given number given the result as 0.
So, additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{9}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{9}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{4}{9}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: {tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{{13}} - \left( {\frac{{ - 7}}{{15}}} \right){/tex} = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{{13}} - \left( {\frac{{ - 7}}{{15}}} \right) = {/tex} {tex}{-6 \over 13}{/tex} + {tex}{7 \over 15}{/tex} 
Taking LCM of 13 and 15 as 195
= {tex}{-6×15 + 7×13 \over 15×13}{/tex} 
= {tex}{-90+91 \over 195}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{{195}}{/tex}

questions.question: Sum of two rational numbers is -8, one of them is {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex}, find the other number.
questions.explanation:
Sum of two rational numbers is -8, one of them is {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} 
Let the other number is x
So, {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} + x = -8
x = - 8 - {tex}\frac{3}{4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-32-3}{4}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{-35}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: How many rational numbers are there between two rational numbers?
questions.explanation: There are infinite numbers between any two rational numbers.

questions.question: Find the sum of {tex}13\frac{3}{4} + \left( { - 11\frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}13\frac{3}{4} + \left( { - 11\frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex} 
Here, we can add 13 + (-11) seperately and {tex}{3 \over 4}{/tex}+ (-{tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex})
So, 13 + (-11) = 2
{tex}{3 \over 4}{/tex}+ (-{tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}) = {tex}{1 \over 4}{/tex} 
So, the ans is {tex}2\frac{1}{4}{/tex}

questions.question: Romila, Pooja and Swati went out for dinner in a hotel. Romila paid {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} of the bill, Pooja paid {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} of the bill. Swati paid the remaining part of the bill. What part of the bill was paid by Swati?
questions.explanation:
Given,
Romila paid {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} of the bill
Pooja paid {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} of the bill
Let, Swati paid bill = x
So, {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}+ {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} + x = 1
x = 1 - {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex} - {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} 
= {tex}{15-3-5 \over 15}{/tex} {taking LCM of 5 and 3 as 15}
= {tex}\frac{7}{{15}}{/tex} 
= {tex}{15-8 \over 15}{/tex}

questions.question: Write down the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{9}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse is the number which when added to the given number gives result as 0.
So, additive inverse of{tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{9}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{9}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{5}{9}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: Write down the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{{11}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse is the number which when added to the given number gives the result as 0.
Additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{{11}}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{{ 4}}{{11}}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{{11}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{{ 4}}{{11}}{/tex}= 0

questions.question: Write down the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{7}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse is the number which when added to the given number gives the result as 0.
Additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{7}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{{ 6}}{7}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{7}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{{ 6}}{7}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: Write down the additive inverse of {tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{{12}}{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Additive inverse is the number which when added to the given number gives the result as 0.
Additive inverse of{tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{{12}}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{{ 7}}{{12}}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{{12}}{/tex} + {tex}\frac{{ 7}}{{12}}{/tex} = 0

questions.question: The equivalent rational number of {tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{5}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
The equivalent rational number of {tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{5}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{{ - 12}}{{10}}{/tex} 
Multiplying both numerator and denominator of {tex}\frac{{ - 6}}{5}{/tex} gives {tex}\frac{{ - 12}}{{10}}{/tex}.

questions.question: Fill in the boxes with the correct symbol: {tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{7}{/tex} ________ {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}{2\over 3}{/tex} is positive and {tex}\frac{{ -5}}{{7}}{/tex} is negative.
So {tex}\frac{{ -5}}{{7}}{/tex} < {tex}{2\over 3}{/tex}

questions.question: Fill in the boxes with the correct symbol: {tex}\frac{{ - 4}}{5}{/tex} ________ {tex}\frac{-5}{7}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{-4 \over 5}{/tex}= -0.8
{tex}{-5\over 7}{/tex} = -0.71
So, clearly, {tex}{-4 \over 5}{/tex}< {tex}{-5\over 7}{/tex}

questions.question: Fill in the boxes with the correct symbol: {tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{8}{/tex} ________ {tex}\frac{14}{-16}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
{tex}{-14\over 16}{/tex}= {tex}{-7 \over 8}{/tex} {reducing {tex}{-14\over 16}{/tex} with 2}
Thus {tex}\frac{{ - 7}}{8}=\frac{{14}}{{ - 16}}{/tex}

questions.question: Fill in the boxes with the correct symbol: {tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{7}{/tex} ________ {tex}\frac{5}{-7}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since both numbers are same with same sign, 
So they are equal.
{tex}\frac{{ - 5}}{7}=\frac{5}{{ - 7}}{/tex}
